The tert-butyl-2-hydrazinyl-2-oxoethylcarbamyl group, when incorporated into melatonin analogs (3k, 3l) via a hydrazone linkage, substantially decreases Abeta42 production in the amyloidogenic pathway.
Leukemia cell lines are more sensitive to S-allylthio-6-mercaptopurine riboside than monolayer cell lines.
CCI52 sensitizes cells to the cytotoxic effects of 6-MP by an alternative mechanism distinct from MRP4 inhibition.
6-phenylthiouridine exhibits anti-leukemic activity in cell cultures.
Cyclophosphamide does not show cell cycle phase specificity for apoptosis, unlike drugs such as etoposide, methotrexate, or cytoside arabinoside, which preferentially induce apoptosis in the S phase.
The cytotoxic effects of 9-aminoacridinecarboxamides are well correlated with the level of drug accumulation in cells, indicating that uptake is a key determinant of their antiproliferative activity.
Cell lines lacking adenosine kinase show varying degrees of resistance to 8-aza-6-mercaptopurine and its derivatives, but are sensitive to the 8-azanucleoside (Z = SH).
o-fluorophenylureidopyrazolo[3,4-d]pyrimidine derivatives exhibit greater antiproliferative activity toward cells of malignant origin compared to normal cells.
N-(5-indanylsulfonyl)-N-(4-chlorophenyl)-urea (ISCU) is equally active against multidrug-resistant human cell lines resistant to anthracyclines and vinca alkaloids.

Combined treatment with [1,2,4]triazolo[4,3-c]quinazoline (NTCHMTQ) at 1–5 µM and cisplatin or temozolomide sensitizes HL-60 leukemia cells, enhancing the efficacy of both drugs as indicated by combination indexes, sub-G0 cell fraction, apoptotic DNA fragmentation, and caspase-3 activity.
3-formyl-2-phenylindoles exhibit cytostatic effects related to tubulin polymerization inhibition.
Trimethylquinoxalate is a lipophilic antifolate that exhibits collateral sensitivity in folate analogue-transport defective tumor cells.
methyl-2-amino-5-((4-isopropylphenylthio)methyl)thiophene-3-carboxylate is a potent and selective anti-T-lymphoma/leukemia agent with an IC50 of 0.3–0.4 µM and a tumor selectivity (TS) of 100–144.
Cyclopropylmethyl pyrazole analogs have demonstrated inhibition of proliferation in an Ewing's Sarcoma cell line, outperforming earlier leads.
3,5-diamino-N-[3-(trifluoromethyl)phenyl]benzamide induces apoptosis in HCT-116 human colon cancer cells and possesses modest pharmacokinetic properties.
N-Hydroxy-4-(4-phenylbutyryl-amino) benzamide induces cell cycle arrest and mitochondria-mediated apoptosis in lung cancer cells.
3-Bromophenylhydrazine is a glycolysis inhibitor that can cooperate with multiple anti-neoplastic agents to alter cellular stress adaptation and sensitivity in leukemia cells.
Amidox induces apoptosis in HL-60 cells, which is correlated with decreased dNTP levels.
Methyl-glyoxal bis(cyclopentylamidinohydrazone) dose-dependently inhibits the growth of human osteosarcoma HuO9 cells by decreasing intracellular spermine, spermidine, and putrescine content.
3-propoxy-6-allylthiopyridazine induces apoptosis in SK-Hep-1 hepatocellular carcinoma cells through a caspase-3-dependent mechanism.
The hydroxystyryl-acrylonitrile compound LS104 inhibits proliferation and induces potent cytotoxic effects in FLT3-expressing leukemic cells in vitro.

Substitution of a meta-phenyl hydrogen atom by a −CH2F moiety in aminobenzoquinolizine inhibitors results in a 400-fold increase in binding affinity to the S1 specificity pocket of DPP-IV, highlighting the importance of hydrophobic interactions and shape complementarity in drug design.
CoMFA analysis of 2-phenyl 4-quinolones shows that size is not critical for tubulin binding in this series.
The 4,5,6,7-tetrahydroindazole moiety is preferred among tested P1 arginine side-chain mimetics for binding in the S1 selectivity pocket of thrombin.
1-n-hexyl substitution in dihydrobenzofuran-7-carboxamides confers high stereospecificity in D2 receptor affinity and inhibitory activity.
Aminothiochromane-based carboxamide ligands are designed to promote hydrogen bonding and van der Waals interactions in the active site of HIV-1 protease.
7-methoxyquinoline-3-carboxamide derivatives are designed to enhance binding to the large active cavity of CETP by substituting phenyl rings with 7-methoxy groups.
pyrimido [5,4-e] pyrrolo [1,2-c] pyrimidine derivatives can be designed to minimize extensive metabolism while forming hydrogen bonds with specific amino acid residues in binding sites.
Replacement of d-Phe at the P3 position with d-diphenylalanine in peptidic thrombin inhibitors results in a dramatic increase in potency.
The hydrophobic ferrocenyl triazole residue is important for maintaining high affinity binding to HIV-1 gp120 in peptide inhibitors.
Aminotetrahydronaphthalene carboxamide derivatives are incorporated as P2 ligands in HIV-1 protease inhibitors to enhance hydrogen bonding interactions with backbone atoms in the S2 subsite.
Ligands with a 1,4-dihydroindeno[1,2-c]pyrazole core exhibit very high binding affinity for CB2 cannabinoid receptors.
The effect of single vs. double bond replacement on binding selectivity for N-(2,3-dichlorophenyl)piperazinyl benzamide analogs is opposite to that observed for N-(2-methoxyphenyl)piperazine analogs.

Brodimoprim analogues with carboxylated alkyl chains at the 3'-O position are designed to improve binding to Lactobacillus casei dihydrofolate reductase by enhancing interactions with basic protein residues.
The basicity of the cyclic amine in 6-ethylpiperidine derivatives does not significantly affect GnRH binding affinity.

Aminopyridinylimidazole derivatives possess flexibility due to independent torsion opportunities of free rotatable C-N-C bonds, providing better conformational tolerance compared to rigid scaffolds like 7-azaindole.
2-thiophenoxy-3-pyrrolidinone analogues possess two possible 'dressing' sites (R1 and R2) that can be used in combinational chemistry to search for more potent anti-HIV agents.

2-(1H-imidazol-1-yl)quinazoline analogs serve as useful templates for the development of potent dihydrofolate reductase (DHFR) inhibitors, with key binding features including hydrophobic and hydrogen bond interactions within the enzyme's active site.
Pyrazolo[1,5-a]pyrimidine derivatives have been structurally modified to improve their physical properties, including solubility, by exploring benzimidazole and related scaffolds.
The methylene spacer length in bisindole-1,3,4-oxadiazole derivatives plays a major role in ligand folding and affects the compound's apoptotic and Bcl-2 binding profile.
Hydrogen-bonding groups at the 7-position of 1,2,3,4-tetrahydrobenz[h]isoquinoline inhibitors are predicted to increase potency against phenethanolamine N-methyltransferase.
Inhibition of catecholamine synthesis by α-methyltyrosine depletes neuronal catecholamine stores and can robustly affect mood in vulnerable patients.
The effect of 4-bromoamphetamine on serotonin depletion in the brain can be reversed by a membrane uptake inhibitor after short periods, but not after 24-48 hours in rats.
PMA potently and selectively inhibits monoamine oxidase A-mediated 5-HT metabolism, enabling it to increase extracellular serotonin levels more effectively than MDMA.
sEH-derived total 7,8-DiHDPE levels increase during the winter months in individuals with major depressive disorder-seasonal (MDD-s) compared to summer-fall.
Replacing N-methylpyrazole with N-ethylpyrazole in the compound structure retains 5-HT 2A receptor binding affinity and increases selectivity for the 5-HT 2A receptor.
4-bromoamphetamine depletes brain S-hydroxyindoles and serotonin, and these effects can last for at least a week in rats.
PMA (3,4-methylenedioxamphetamine) exerts its effects predominantly through serotonergic mechanisms, with greater effects on 5-HT release and reuptake than on dopamine.
Elevated DOPAC:DA ratios indicate diminished vesicular packaging and increased cytosolic dopamine turnover.
Homologation of the 5-methoxy substituent of 2,4,5-trimethoxyamphetamine to an ethoxy group results in a marked loss of pharmacological activity as a 5-HT 2 agonist.
Treatment with 4-chloro-DL-phenylalanine ethyl ester HCl at 150 mg/kg/day for 3 days in rats results in a 70% reduction of cortical 5-hydroxytryptophan content.
2,4,5-trimethoxycinnamic acid, a component of Soyo, exerts antidepressant effects in mice in forced swimming tests.
Kainite induces seizures in rats mediated by calcium influx through NMDA receptor-linked and voltage sensitive calcium channels.
N-methyl-d-l-aspartate directly affects motoneuron membrane potential in the lumbar spinal cord, influencing synaptic drive and locomotor activity.
A dose of pentetrazol that produces solely clonic seizures does not serve as a primary screen for new compounds in anticonvulsant activity assays.
Agonist activity of capsaicin analogues in vitro is predictive of antinociceptive activity in vivo.
AY-9944 treatment in rats is associated with ictal EEG activity in the hippocampus, a feature not observed in models for typical absence seizures.
Acamprosate tablets have been used clinically for about 20 years to maintain abstinence in alcohol-dependent patients.
Potassium N-acetylaminosuccinate (Cogitum) is superior to placebo in the treatment of fatigue (asthenic syndrome).
N-(2-chloroethyl)-N-2-bromobenzylamine hydrochloride (DSP-4) is a specific and selective lesioning agent for locus coeruleus neurons.
In the SarCNU i.a. group, protein synthesis in the bilateral frontal cortices shows a significant decrease on Day 3 compared to Day 0, as measured by protein synthesis rate.

FTY720 is an immunomodulator that acts as a potent immunosuppressive agent after kidney transplantation in rodents and humans when combined with subtherapeutic concentrations of cyclosporin A, FK506, or rapamycin.

OKY-046 is a specific thromboxane synthetase inhibitor that, when administered to rats with kidney allografts, enables complete preservation of renal function for four days in an acute renal allograft rejection model without other immunosuppressive therapy.
Mycophenolic acid is the active metabolite of the immunosuppressive agent mycophenolate mofetil.
OKY-046, as a thromboxane antagonist, may serve as a potential adjunctive therapy for the treatment of acute renal allograft rejection.
Malononitrilamide FK778 has been investigated as an antirejection drug with potential roles in preventing allograft and xenograft rejection in rodent and dog models.
Liposome-encapsulated dichloroethylene diphosphanate eliminates Kupffer cells in the donor liver, which improves graft function and reduces cytokine production after liver transplantation.
1,2,4-triazolo [4,3-c]pyrimidine derivatives suppress IL-2 production by peripheral blood mononuclear cells and whole blood.
Nitromethyl imidazole is a nonenzymatically cleaved initial byproduct of azathioprine metabolism that is linked to gastrointestinal intolerance.
Prodipine is an irreversible inhibitor of DP IV that prevents the increase in serum DP IV activity normally observed during the first days after cardiac allotransplantation in rats.
WIN 17317-3 is a potent blocker of voltage-gated sodium channels, which may affect its immunosuppressant activity in whole animal models.
(R)-3-methyl morpholine, when incorporated into triazine-based mTOR inhibitors, maintains potency comparable to bis-3,5-bridged morpholine derivatives while improving human microsomal stability.
NEP treatment is associated with reduced tissue interstitial fibrosis (about 5%) and less graft injury compared to UW and UFH groups at the end of a 3-month follow-up.

Prodipine is an irreversible inhibitor of dipeptidyl peptidase IV (DP IV/CD26) that, when administered in a rat transplantation model, prevents the increase in serum DP IV activity and delays allograft rejection and tissue DP IV activity during the initial days after cardiac allotransplantation.
Haptenated red cell-treated pigs are highly tolerant to I and its congeners throughout an 8-week study period.
Pralidoxime does not exhibit overt immune toxicity or modulation in in vitro assays at concentrations relevant to plasma levels after pretreatment.

Therapeutic administration of 4-acetyl-1-(4-chlorophenyl)-3-(2-methylthiophenyl)-3-pyrazolin-5-one prevents the development of autoimmune renal disease in the chronic GvH model of systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) and in NZB/W Fl mice with lupus-like disease.

4-acetyl-1-(4-chlorophenyl)-3-(2-methylthiophenyl)-3-pyrazolin-5-one, also known as BTS 71 412, is a potent immunosuppressant that suppresses both cell-mediated and antibody-mediated immune reactions when administered orally.
2-Amino-2-{2-[octylphenyl]ethyl}-1,3-propanediol (FTY-720) reduces ischemia-reperfusion injury (IRI) in kidney and liver tissues in vivo.
Resveratrol suppresses T-cell expansion and pro-inflammatory cytokine production in vivo and in vitro.
2-bromo-1-(6-bromo-3-methylthiazolo[3,2-a]benzimidazol-2-yl) ethanone is a potent immunosuppressor against both macrophages and T-lymphocytes.
6-fluoro benzothiazole, as present in compound 6a, contributes to good antibacterial activity against M. tuberculosis with MIC values in the range of 50–62.5 mg/ml.
Mn(II) complexes of [2-((3-(benzyloxy)pyridin-2-ylimino)methyl)phenol] (Bpmp) inhibit the growth of both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria to a competent level.

Bis(acrylate)-bis(2-ethylimidazole)-copper(II) demonstrates broad in vitro antimicrobial activity against Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria and fungal strains of both clinical and ecological importance.
A methylsubstituted pentanamide at the C-5 position is required for the antimicrobial activity of agrocin 84.

Cyanopyrroloazepine derivatives synthesized via Fe3O4/CuO/ZnO@MWCNT-catalyzed multicomponent reactions possess NH groups and exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activity, including inhibition of both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria.

5,10,15,20-tetrakis(4-methoxyphenyl-3-sulfonatophenyl) porphyrin (AgTMPPS) exhibits strong antibacterial activity against MRSA and can be used for non-invasive bacterial sterilization via photodynamic therapy involving metal ion and photodynamic effects.

Bismuth-copper sulfide nanocomposites (BiZ/CuxS NCs) exhibit broad-spectrum antibacterial activity, including efficacy against multidrug-resistant bacteria and biofilms, due to surface vacancies and heterojunctions that enhance phototherapeutic response upon near-infrared irradiation.
5-carbomethoxymethyl-7-hydroxy-2-pentylchromone exhibits antimicrobial activity against tested microbial strains and shows moderate antioxidant property.

4-phenylbenzimidazole is a core structure in aryl thioether analogues for M. tuberculosis activity, with ethyl at the 2-position and methyl at the 6-position on the benzimidazole ring being favored substituents.
3-(quinoxalin-3-yl)prop-2yn-1-ol exhibits anticancer activity against human A549 lung cancer cells and shows promising antimycobacterial activity against the Mtb H 37 R V strain.
The ethylenediamine derivative 20 exhibits higher antimalarial activity compared to other tested derivatives, likely due to its higher conformational flexibility.
Modifications at the azetidine nitrogen can influence the potency of antimalarial azetidine-containing compounds, with EC50 values decreasing in the order H > alkyl > carbamate.
Nickel(II) furanylmethyl dithiolene is a dithiolate complex with significant antibacterial and antifungal activity.
Substitution of the aromatic amino group with a 2,2,2-trichloroethyl-carboxylate moiety in ergoline analogues confers antifungal activity against C. neoformans.
The phenylhexafluoroisopropanol moiety is essential for high in vitro maltose-binding protein (MCD) inhibitory activity.

Bornyl coumarate, a compound with both phenylpropanoid and monoterpene moieties, exhibits potent activity against a standard Staphylococcus aureus (SA) strain with a MIC of 0.6 µg/ml, likely due to its lipophilic monoterpene portion facilitating membrane permeability.

Silver-bismuth nanoparticles supported on mesoporous silica (Ag-Bi@SiO2 NPs) exhibit synergistic antibacterial activity and photothermal-enhanced efficacy against methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA), including biofilm disruption and high in vivo bacterial clearance in abscesses.
1-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxybenzyl)isatin serves as a precursor for synthesizing isatin-3-hydrazone derivatives with antioxidant and antibacterial properties.
Copper thiazole is used as a positive control for antibacterial activity in in vivo assays at a concentration of 50 mg/L.
N,N-dimethyl-8-chloro-5-methoxy-1,2,3,4-tetrahydro-1-naphthylamine competitively blocks β-adrenergic receptors in anesthetized dogs and in isolated aortic strips.

Dietary administration of sulfaminouracil to rats with encapsulated kidneys prevents the rise of systolic blood pressure to levels seen in untreated controls and reduces or prevents cardiac hypertrophy associated with renal hypertension.
Daily treatment with 150 mg/kg GVG does not influence the development of hypertension in SHR or blood pressure in WKY.
GVG treatment does not modify arterial blood pressure in hypertensive (SHR) or normotensive (WKY) rats.
LNP 509 produces hypotensive and bradycardic effects in spontaneously hypertensive rats, accompanied by a significant decrease in cardiac output and a decrease in systemic vascular resistance.

2-Amino-dihydro-6-methyl-4H-1,3-thiazine bolus treatment modifies baroreflex sensitivity for heart rate in suspended rats by decreasing the curvature coefficient of the mean arterial pressure response, indicating expanded MAP response range, and increasing baroreceptor input by expanding the heart rate range.

Infusion of 2-Amino-dihydro-6-methyl-4H-1,3-thiazine enhances the baroreflex response, as evidenced by an increased shift of the baroreflex curve to the right and increases in both the MAP 50 value and the gain.
Indanyloxyacetic acid hyperpolarizes and relaxes isolated arterioles in a concentration-dependent manner.
2,4-dihydro-3H [1,2,4]triazole-3-thione derivatives with a shorter connecting chain show increased affinity for alpha-1 adrenoceptor sites compared to their structural isomers.
HBBPC likely induces vasoconstriction in some areas, leading to increased femoral blood flow and decreased peripheral resistance without a drop in blood pressure.
DMA has potency for inhibiting the hypertensive effect of DOCA-salt treatment that is almost equal to that of benzamil, despite having much lower potency for Naϩ channels and Naϩ/Hϩ exchange.
O-alkylation of betaxolol with a cyclopentylmethyl group results in an increase in plasma protein binding to 88.2% when using 0.03 M acetate buffer (pH 5.6)-acetonitrile (60:40) as the mobile phase.
Nitroarginine hydrochloride at 5 mg/kg decreases cCBF without elevating systemic blood pressure in vivo.
GVG delays the increase in body weight in both hypertensive and normotensive rats.
Inhalation of DMHF significantly decreases renal sympathetic nerve activity (RSNA), suggesting it may decrease blood pressure by suppressing renin secretion and renal sympathetic nerve activity.

DL-serine-N 2 isopropylhydrazide decreases total peripheral resistance and produces a hypotensive effect without altering cardiac output, blood volume, sodium space, renal plasma flow, or glomerular filtration rate.

7-Chloro-4-methoxy-1-indanone is a compound whose P-adrenoceptor blocking potency can be estimated in vivo by measuring its ability to inhibit tachycardia induced by a submaximal dose of isoproterenol (0.2 µg/kg iv) in cats.
Iso-propylindazole demonstrates the highest selectivity for β 3-AR over α 1A-AR among the tested indazole compounds.
Azulene-1-carboxamidine derivative HNS-32 exhibits vasorelaxant and selective inhibition of cerebral artery contraction mediated via activation of protein kinase C.

Administration of Glyceryl dinitrophenol increases resting heart rate from 64 ± 3 bpm to 107 ± 2 bpm and mean arterial pressure (MAP) from 90 ± 2 mmHg to 102 ± 3 mmHg compared to control baseline in the described experimental condition.
Within the therapeutic range of 0.05–0.17 mg/L, quetiapine hydrochloride has an incidence of extrapyramidal symptoms that does not differ significantly from placebo.
5-[2-[4-(2,3-dimethyl-phenyl)-piperazin-1-yl]ethyl]1H-benzotriazole does not induce the cataleptic behavioural pattern associated with atypical antipsychotics.
Haloperidol has clinically insignificant anticholinergic and antihistamine effects.

4-(4-chlorophenyl)-1-[4-(4-fluorophenyl)-4-oxobutyl] pyridinium (HPP+) may contribute to the development of tardive dyskinesia, parkinsonism, and other late extrapyramidal syndromes by interacting with neurotransmitter transporters.
Racemic amisulpride induces catalepsy in rats only at very high doses (>100 mg/kg, s.c.), with the maximal latency time observed after 300 min and lasting for at least 1 h.
Both enantiomers of 5-methoxy-2-[N-(2-benzamidoethyl)-N-n-propylamino]tetralin lack cataleptogenic activity in rats, indicating a low risk of inducing extrapyramidal side effects.

At high doses, 7-chloro-10-(4-methyl-1-piperazinyl)-thieno[3,2-b][1,5]benzothiazepine is not cataleptogenic and only weakly antagonizes amphetamine- or apomorphine-induced stereotyped behavior in rats.
At low doses, 7-chloro-10-(4-methyl-1-piperazinyl)-thieno[3,2-b][1,5]benzothiazepine antagonizes amphetamine-induced hypermotility in mice.
(SÀ)-amisulpride at 30 mg/kg induces intense catalepsy in rats after 5 h from administration, as measured by the bar test.

Chronic schizophrenic patients treated with conventional neuroleptics have decreased affinity for (S)-3-[123I]iodo-5,6-dimethoxyl-N-[(1-ethyl-2-pyrrolidinyl)methyl] -salicylamide (123I-NCQ298) in the basal ganglia, with values in the range of 10–60% of the median value for untreated controls.
Dantrolene Na is used as a treatment for neuroleptic malignant syndrome (NMS), but in this case, it was ineffective in clearing persistent symptoms despite co-administration with other drugs.
N,N-diphenyl-dopamine analogues do not induce asymmetric postures in caudectomized mice.
A 6-phenyl substituent on the 1-phenylbenzazepine scaffold does not affect D1 receptor binding affinity.
SCH 23390 is a selective dopamine D2 receptor antagonist.
(R+)-amisulpride does not induce catalepsy in rats up to a dose of 75 mg/kg in the bar test.
Haloperidol can bind to D1 dopamine, 5-HT2 serotonin, H1 histamine, and α2 adrenergic receptors in the brain.
Para-amino tyrosine is used experimentally to deplete dopamine in patients with schizophrenia, which can induce dysphoric reactions in those with a history of neuroleptic dysphoria.
Quinolyl-piperazinyl piperidine analogues can act as potent and selective 5-HT(1A) antagonists.
Phenylbutyrophenone derivatives function as antagonists at the 5-HT 2A, 5-HT 2C, and DA D 2 receptors, with selectivity profiles influenced by substituents at the phenyl ring.
2-(Aminomethyl)-4-phenylpyrrolo[1,2-a]quinoxalines possess central dopamine antagonist activity.

A physiologically based pharmacokinetic (PBPK) model of dorzagliatin was developed and validated using clinical data, supporting its mechanistic understanding and application in drug-drug interaction studies.
TDM enables the presence of insulin in plasma at levels above baseline (>100 uU/ml) at 60, 120, and 180 minutes after nasal administration of mixed insulin formulations.
Tofogliflozin improves glycemic control in diabetic rats and mice.
Bis(isotridecyl)sulfosuccinate forms hydrophobic ion pairs with insulin glargine (IG) that exhibit the highest log P and improved dissociation profile among tested counterions.
Fusion of insulin to a fluorenylmethyloxycarbonyl derivative of 16-sulfanyl hexadecenoic acid results in a 5-fold increase in plasma half-life of insulin after IV administration in rat models.
Ornithine-keto glutarate administration in human subjects elicits insulin secretion.
A prolonged (20 min) infusion of 1 mM kisspeptin-13 does not significantly affect basal insulin secretion.
N-AcGIP exerts glucose-dependent stimulatory actions on alpha (A) cells in type 2 diabetes animal models.
Lobeglitazone, when combined with metformin, provides beneficial efficacy and a favorable safety profile for patients with type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM).

TDM facilitates rapid insulin absorption from the nasal cavity when included in mixed insulin formulations, with Tmax of 15 minutes and sustained elevated insulin concentrations for more than 180 minutes.
Growth hormone does not significantly stimulate 3-O-methyl-glucose uptake in diaphragms from untreated mice aged 11–40 weeks.
The efficacious dose range of CMPD-A relative to sildenafil can be estimated using PK/PD modeling and penile plethysmography data.
At 2.8 mM glucose and extracellular Ca2+, 1-[4-(5-chloro-2-methoxybenzamidoethyl)benzene sulfonamido]-1-cyclohexylamino-2-nitroethylene stimulates insulin output in rat pancreatic islets.

CrGly, a chromium(III) complex, modulates the number of insulin receptors accessible to insulin, their affinity to the hormone, and the phosphorylation state of insulin signaling proteins such as IRS-1 in rat liver membranes in vitro.
Dextrorphan O-glucuronide is a metabolite of dextromethorphan, and its plasma concentration can be modeled and predicted using a PBPK approach that includes CYP2D6 activity.
2,3,7,7-tetrachlorodibenzo-p-dioxin (TCDD) exposure decreases glucose uptake by adipose tissue and pancreas and impairs insulin secretion.
Efaroxane functions as a K ATP channel opener and stimulates insulin secretion in INS-1 cells.

The glucose-lowering effects of exendin-4, a GLP-1 receptor agonist, are markedly potentiated by (3S)-3-ethoxy-3-(4-{[(1R)-4-(trifluoromethyl)-2,3-dihydro-1H-inden-1-yl]oxy}phenyl)propanoic acid (DS-1558) at 3 mg/kg during an intraperitoneal glucose tolerance test in diabetic db/db mice.
Simulated free cardiac NT concentrations are used as inputs for cardiac sodium channel blocking (CSS) modeling of PD when comparing PBPK model predictions with observed plasma concentrations.

The pyrrolidin-2-ylmethyl amide moiety is relevant for the structure-activity relationship (SAR) of DPP-4 inhibitors, with modifications to this group influencing potency and in vitro ADME properties.
Saframycin-A is a potent anticancer alkaloid that can be synthesized using small aromatic analogues of phenanthroquinolizidines as intermediates.
(+)-gigantecin is a rare nonadjacent bis-THF-containing acetogenin and a potent cytotoxic antitumor agent.
(S)-autumnaline is the substrate for the subsequent CYP-catalyzed para-para phenol coupling reaction in the biosynthetic pathway leading to colchicine.
(S)-autumnaline is a precursor in colchicine biosynthesis that undergoes phenol-phenol coupling with isoandrocymbine, a reaction potentially catalyzed by cytochrome P450 enzymes.
Hydroxycinnamoylglucose is an intermediate in the biosynthesis of chlorogenic acid (3-O-caffeoylquinic acid) in Ipomoea via an alternative route to caffeoyl-CoA-dependent biosynthesis.
(R)-1-(5,8-Dihydroxy-1,4-dioxo-1,4-dihydronaphthalen-2-yl)-4-methylpent-3-enyl cyclopropanecarboxylate (compound 3j) is a shikonin ester derivative that acts as a potent anticancer agent.
Biosynthetic pathways for ravidomycin V have been proposed based on comparative analysis of NMR data.
Chloromaloside A exhibits in vitro cytotoxicity against several human cancer cell lines.
(-)-stephacidin B is a unique dimeric alkaloid with potent biological activity, biosynthesized by dimerization of (+)-avrainvillamide.
1-deoxypaclitaxel analogues can be synthesized from the naturally occurring compound 1-deoxybaccatin VI via selective deacylation at specific acetyl positions.
Physalin C is a proteasome-targeted anticancer agent.
Arugosin I is proposed as a biosynthetic precursor of arugosin N and 1,6,10-trihydroxy-8-methyl-2-(3-methyl-2-butenyl)-dibenz[b,e]oxepin-11(6H)-one.
10-methoxycamptothecin was isolated from the CHCl3 extract of the plant material.
4-Deoxypodophyllotoxin exhibits potent cytotoxicity against a wide variety of tumor cell lines.
Aklavinone is a key intermediate in the biosynthesis of most anthracyclines.
Hopeanol shares the same biosynthetic pathway as hopeanol B, serving as a common precursor for hopeahainols A and B via a presumed intermediate 4a.
N-methylmaysenine is a maytansinoid for which the core structure and a key intermediate have been synthesized via a route involving the vinylogous Mukaiyama aldol reaction and macrolactonization.
(S)-autumnaline is an intermediate in the biosynthetic pathway from dopamine and phenol aldehyde to colchicine, formed via a Pictet-Spengler and oxidative phenol-phenol coupling.
3β-formyloxyresibufogenin displays significant inhibitory activity against cancer cell lines.
Stephacidin B represents a new level of complexity within prenylated indole alkaloids from fungi.
Tetrahydroquinoline-pyrimidine potently inhibits tumor growth in an A375 melanoma xenograft model.
Nexturastat A inhibits the growth of B16 melanoma cells and demonstrates antiproliferative effects.
Zoligratinib resistance can be promoted by certain FGFR2 mutations.
Erasin is a RAS-selective lethal compound that induces ferroptosis.
Gold resveratrol hollow nanoparticles, when exposed to 808 nm laser, induce cell cycle arrest, apoptosis, and cell death in A375 melanoma cells.

5-(3-chloro-4-trifluoromethoxy phenyl amino)-2-hydroxy-3-undecyl- [1, 4] benzoquinone (EOCF) induces apoptosis, cell cycle arrest at G0/G1 phase, and inhibits both cell proliferation and metastatic potential in melanoma cell lines.
N-(4-(1,3,2-dithiarsinan-2-yl)phenyl)acrylamide (AAZ2) inhibits the growth of gastric cancer xenograft tumors in vivo.
6-phosphoguanosine diphosphate (6PGD) expression and activity are upregulated in renal cell carcinoma (RCC) patients and cells, contributing to tumorigenesis and resistance.
Braftide allosterically inhibits BRAF activation by disrupting BRAF homodimer formation, as shown by decreased BRAF homodimer formation at 50 µM in HEK293 cell lysates.
Spd-CoA blocks DNA damage repair pathways and sensitizes cells to chemotherapeutic drugs and UV-radiation.
TMPyP4 inhibits telomerase through interaction with G-quadruplex DNA.
2-naphthoylethyltrimethylammonium (alpha-NETA) treatment induces pyroptosis in ovarian cancer by cleaving GSDMD.
The melanogenesis inhibitory effect of camellioside F in B16 melanoma 4A5 cells is partly related to its proliferation inhibitory effect on these cells.
(Z)-3,4,5,4-tetramethoxystilbene inhibits EGFR phosphorylation and activation in gefitinib-resistant lung cancer cells.

6-methoxy-1H-indol-3-yl-(3,4,5-trimethoxyphenyl)methane derivatives inhibit tubulin polymerization and show nanomolar IC50 values against the B16 melanoma cell line (e.g., 13, 45, 24, and 23 nM for compounds 25b-27b and 35b, respectively).
Aspartyltryptophan is identified as a key small molecule in colorectal cancer (CRC) pathogenesis based on multi-platform metabolome analysis.
N-Hydroxy-4-(4-phenylbutyryl-amino) benzamide disrupts F-actin dynamics by downregulating RhoA activity in lung cancer cells.
6-iodobenzo[a]pyrene does not exhibit tumor-initiating or carcinogenic activity in mouse skin.

Braftide inhibits ectopically expressed p61 BRAFV600E, a spliced form of BRAFV600E that constitutively dimerizes in a RAS-independent manner, by blocking the scaffolding function of p61 BRAFV600E rather than inhibiting its kinase activity.
NIK-SMI1 inhibits NIK activity, leading to suppressed LIGHT responses in esophageal fibroblasts.
3-oxalomalate is genetically controlled and its levels are predictive of ALT elevation due to therapeutic acetaminophen.
L-nitromethylarginine inhibits apoptosis and NOS activity induced by cyclosporine in renal cell lines.
L-nitromethylarginine at 0.8 mmol/L inhibits LPS-induced nitric oxide release in primary fetal hepatocytes after 5 hours of stimulation.
The cytotoxicity of 6-fluoro-8-nitro-4-oxo-1,4-dihydroquinoline-3-carboxylate is mediated via activation of caspase-9 and caspase-3, leading to apoptosis.
Rhoifolin, a flavanone glycoside, provides virtually complete protection against okadaic acid-induced autophagy inhibition in isolated rat hepatocytes.
Hexa-D-arginine amide inhibits Pseudomonas aeruginosa exotoxin A (PEA)-mediated toxicity.
Gallate pretreatment suppresses cytokine-induced mRNA and cell-surface expression of VCAM-1, ICAM-1, and E-selectin in human umbilical vein endothelial cells.
Diglucosyltetrahydrocurcumin shows fine dose dependency in inhibiting histamine release from RBL-2H3 cells induced by concanavalin A.

Benzoindoloquinoxaline derivatives have been synthesized and assessed for anti-VSV activity, interferon-inducing ability, and cytotoxicity.

14-epicochlioquinone B exhibits cytotoxic and antimicrobial activities.

Isocyanide 1 is a potential antifouling compound designed from the structure-function relationship of marine natural products.
Forbesione exhibits antibacterial activity and cytotoxicity, which may be related to its structural scaffold.

Single intravenous administration of 9H-xanthene-9-carboxylic acid {2,2,2-trichloro-1-[3-(3-cyano-phenyl)-thioureido]-ethyl}-amide (Xc) to high-fat diet-induced diabetic mice stimulates AMPK phosphorylation in skeletal muscle and improves glucose tolerance.

4H-pyridopyrimidine analogs exhibit broad-spectrum inhibition of bacterial growth.

5-Dodecyloxymethyl-2'deoxyuridine derivatives with various substituents on the sugar moiety exhibit antimycobacterial activity depending on the length of the substituent at the C5 position.
4'-ethenylthymidine exhibits potent anti-HSV-1 and anti-HIV-1 activities with no significant cytotoxicity.

The 5-cyano-3,4-dihydroxytetrahydrofuran ring scaffold is associated with the clinical activity of remdesivir against SARS-CoV-2.
The quinolone pyrimidine scaffold is a key feature that confers higher potency in the development of NDH-2 inhibitors for Mycobacterium tuberculosis.
Quinoazolinediamine compounds are among the most potent antibacterials identified, exhibiting EC50 values less than 0.5 µM against E. coli in a turbidometric assay.
2,8-bis(dibutylaminomethyl)phenazine exhibits antitubercular activity in mice.
2,8-bis(dibutylaminomethyl)phenazine exhibits antitubercular activity in mice.
2'-C-hydroxymethyl adenosine exhibits potent anti-HCV activity.
Fluorine substitution at position 3 of 2-(butyldisulfanyl) quinazolin-4(3H)-one confers strong antibacterial activity with an EC50 of 12 μg/mL.
7-[4-((3-hydroxy-4-oxo-4H-pyran-2-yl)methyl)piperazin-1-yl]quinolone derivatives have been synthesized and evaluated for antibacterial activity.

Dibenzo[a,g]quinolizin-7-ium derivatives with a biphenyl substituent at either the 2- or 12-position exhibit significantly enhanced antibacterial potency against Staphylococcus aureus and Enterococcus faecalis.
2-beta-D-ribofuranosyl-1,2,4-oxadiazole-3,5-dione exhibits significant antiviral activity against herpes simplex virus type 1 in vitro.
Inhibition of Panx 1 with CBX has a potential effect on alleviating neuropathic pain.

Sodium, calcium, and potassium (fast and slow) ion currents in Lymnaea stagnalis neurons are reversibly and dose-dependently inhibited by opioid analgesics, resulting in reduced nonspecific leak currents and membrane stabilization.

NA increases I Kir and decreases I h, with effects on I h and instantaneous current that are poorly reversible and persist for at least 5-10 minutes after wash-out.

Sodium busulfan promotes apoptosis in the rat testis by increasing caspase3 activity.
Germanium sesquioxide has been associated with significant dose-related nephrotoxicity and neurotoxicity in clinical studies.
Homologation of the 5-methoxy substituent of 2,4,5-trimethoxyamphetamine to an ethoxy group results in a marked loss of pharmacological activity as a 5-HT 2 agonist.

Protein binding of carboxymethylpropyl-furanpropionic acid hampers its removal during dialysis.
Renal damage in male rats is not caused by a single intraperitoneal dose of (S)-α-chlorohydrin at 100 mg/kg.

Urinary 2,3,4,5-Tetrachloro-1-cyclohexanecarboxylic acid (TDCCA) levels show no association with dichotomized semen parameters in logistic regression models.
A 5'-sulfonamide substituted derivative of 2-phenyl pyrido[1,2-e]purin-4(3H)-one exhibits potent and selective inhibitory activity toward PDE5.

Inhibition of catecholamine synthesis by α-methyltyrosine depletes neuronal catecholamine stores and can robustly affect mood in vulnerable patients.
Perinatal exposure to 3,4,3,4-tetrachlorobenzoate at 0.1 mg/(kgrday) elevates regional brain dopamine concentrations in the absence of significant changes in offspring body weight.
AMPT is a medication that has been associated with side effects such as sedation, transient anxiety (lasting 30–60 min), insomnia persisting 1–2 days after discontinuation, and crystaluria.

1-(1-m-nitrophenylcyclohexyl)piperidine has much lower potency than 1-(1-m-amino-phenylcyclohexyl)piperidine for depressing the polysynaptic reflex in neonatal rat spinal cord in vitro.

Amitriptyline is administered at a single dose of 75 mg at 8 p.m., with a maintenance dose of 150 mg (75 mg twice daily) for 6 weeks in patients with severe depression as assessed by the Hamilton Rating Scale for Depression (HRS) score ≥16.

4-(4-chlorophenyl)-1-[4-(4-fluorophenyl)-4-oxobutyl] pyridinium (HPP+) may contribute to the development of tardive dyskinesia, parkinsonism, and other late extrapyramidal syndromes by interacting with neurotransmitter transporters.
In utero and lactational exposure to 3,4,3,4-tetrachlorobenzoate significantly increases dopamine concentrations in the frontal cortex.

Manganese deposition in the brain can occur after administration for as little as 20 days, and after periods up to 126 months, depending on the dose and frequency of administration.

4-bromoamphetamine depletes brain S-hydroxyindoles and serotonin, and these effects can last for at least a week in rats.
The enantiomers of nornicotine differ in their behavioral and pharmacological activities.
Perinatal exposure to 3,4,3,4-tetrachlorobenzoate at 1 mg/(kgrday) significantly reduces subsequent growth in offspring.
7-oxo-4,7-dihydropyrazolo[1,5-a]pyrimidine-3-carboxylic acid derivatives are potent in vitro angiotensin II (AII) antagonists but lack oral antihypertensive activity.
Pyrazolo[1,5-a]pyrimidine derivatives have been structurally modified to improve their physical properties, including solubility, by exploring benzimidazole and related scaffolds.
2,4-dihydro-3H [1,2,4]triazole-3-thione derivatives with a shorter connecting chain show increased affinity for alpha-1 adrenoceptor sites compared to their structural isomers.

The 3-hydroxyphenyl group in piperidinone-based fXa inhibitors does not affect factor Xa inhibitory activity, allowing its use as a handle for introducing solubilizing groups.
The thioacetate methyl ester prodrug derivative of compound 6m acts as an orally active and potent inhibitor of endothelin-converting enzyme-1 (ECE-1) activity in rats.
Hydrophobic contacts via 3-chloro groups on the imidazo[1,2-a]pyridine ring significantly contribute to binding affinity at B 2 bradykinin receptors.

Functionalization at the C-3 position of the indane nucleus with phenylcyanoguanidine side chains produces derivatives with antiischemic potency and cardioselectivity superior to CRK and comparable to benzopyran derivatives.

N-propylpiperidin-2-one esters of ketorolac and ketoprofen are more effective than their parent drugs in enhancing transdermal penetration, though enzymatic activity limitations may affect their efficacy.
2-aminoimidazol-4-one derivatives have been optimized to improve pIC50 values and Caco-2 permeability.
2-n-butyl-4-aminoquinazoline derivatives generally yielded more potent and balanced dual alpha-1 and AII receptor antagonists than 4-quinazolinone derivatives.
2-methylbenzoylsulfonamide is well tolerated as a substituent on the triazolinone scaffold for AT1 receptor antagonism.
Replacement of the amide bond with an oxadiazole ring in 5-(1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-1,2,4-oxadiazoles resolves hydrolytic instability and improves potency.

(±)-trans-[4-[[2-(1,1′-biphenyl-4-yl)-2-(1H-imidazol-1-ylmethyl)-1,3-dioxolan-4-yl]methylthio]phenyl]carbamic acid ethyl ester (2b) causes less severe damage to cytoskeletal F-actin filaments in GL15 cells compared to 2a.

2-chloro-N-(2-(3,4-dimethoxyphenyl)benzo[d]oxazol-5-yl)benzamide (BXZ3) exhibits an improved gastric safety profile compared to other active COX inhibitors.

3-n-amylpyrazole exhibits more favorable therapeutic indices (T.I.'s) in rats than in mice.

Urinary tract toxicity from (R)-N-(2-[4- (carboxymethyl)phenoxy]ethyl)-N-(beta-hydroxyphenethyl)ammonium chloride in rats is associated with reduced red blood cell indices, lower platelet counts, and higher white cell counts.

Urinary tract toxicity from (R)-N-(2-[4- (carboxymethyl)phenoxy]ethyl)-N-(beta-hydroxyphenethyl)ammonium chloride in rats is associated with reduced red blood cell indices, lower platelet counts, and higher white cell counts.

The t-Hpp1 analogue of the delta opioid receptor selective tetrapeptide displays delta receptor binding affinity similar to the parent Tyr1-containing peptide.

2,4-dihydro-3H [1,2,4]triazole-3-thione derivatives with a shorter connecting chain exhibit lower affinity for the 5-HT 1A receptor compared to their structural isomers.

2,4-dihydro-3H-[1,2,4]triazole-3-thione derivatives have been tested for binding affinity and selectivity at the 5-HT 1A receptor and the α1-adrenoceptor.

2,4-dihydro-3H [1,2,4]triazole-3-thione derivatives with a shorter connecting chain show increased affinity for alpha-1 adrenoceptor sites compared to their structural isomers.
