Repeated administration of 2,5-dimethoxy-4-iodoamphetamine hydrobromide reverses the decline in PPT mRNA levels in striatal subregions after 6-hydroxydopamine-induced dopamine depletion.
3-aminoethyl-1-tetralone compounds have been synthesized and their binding affinities to D2, 5-HT2A, and 5-HT2C receptors have been explored.

5-methoxy-b-carboline analogues are the highest affinity methoxy-substituted derivatives for 5-HT 2C receptors, with K i =75 nM, and show enhanced affinity compared to their unsubstituted parent compounds.
Some 5-amino-3-(pyrrolidin-2-ylmethyl)-1H-indole derivatives are highly selective agonists for the 5-HT 6 receptor over several other 5-HT and dopamine receptors.
2-fluoro-5-methoxybenzyl piperazine derivatives exhibit sub-nanomolar affinity for the 5-HT 1A receptor.

The (R)-2-aminopentane derivative of lysergamide has comparable affinity for the ketanserin-labeled 5-HT2A receptor to LSD, but its in vivo activity in the drug discrimination assay drops off compared to LSD.
The PRL response to DOI in rats is thought to be 5-HT 2A-mediated.
Benzofuro [7, 6 -g]quinoline is a structural skeleton to which new 5-HT 1A receptor agonist ligands have been added.
Agonist activity of capsaicin analogues in vitro is predictive of antinociceptive activity in vivo.
2-hydroxyisophthalic acid exhibits analgesic activity in rats.
(-)-spectaline is inactive in the hyperalgesic model of formalin-induced pain in mice.
(-)-spectaline inhibits capsaicin-induced neurogenic pain in mice with an ID50 of 20.81 µg/paw.
Analogues of endomorphin-2 containing (2S)-2-methyl-3-(2,6-dimethyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propanoic acid as the N-terminal amino group are moderately potent mu opioid antagonists.
Planar N-thianaphthylmethyl analogues of (+)-(3R,4R)-dimethyl-4-(3-hydroxyphenyl)piperidine have significantly lower binding affinities for the opioid receptor compared to non-planar analogues.
Nucleoamino acid analogues of enkephalins containing thyminyl alanine exhibit antinociceptive activity in mice when administered either intracisternally or intravenously.

(+)-3f, a 1,4-dihydropyridine derivative, acts as an effective and selective N-type voltage-dependent calcium channel (VDCC) blocker with potent analgesic efficacy in rat formalin-induced pain models.
(Z)-3-(5-ethyl-4-hydroxy-3-methoxy-1-naphthalenyl)-2-methyl-2-propeno ic acid shows potent antiinflammatory effects in animal models of inflammation.
Pyridothiazine-1,1-dioxide derivatives with an arylpiperazinylpropyl moiety at position 2 exhibit analgesic activity in a mouse writhing syndrome test.
The antinociceptive effect of 3-methyl-5-hydroxy-5-trichloromethyl-1H-1-pyrazolcarboxyamide (MPCA) in the formalin test is not mediated by opioid receptors, as naloxone does not prevent its effect.
Azidothiorphan produces long-lasting naloxone-reversible analgesia after intracerebroventricular administration.
N-(6-methylpyridin-2-yl)propionamide exhibits 98% inhibition of acetic acid-induced writhing and very low ulcerogenic effects.
HO-α-sanshool induces firing in both tactile and cool-sensitive neurons and increases sensitivity to innocuous stimuli in previously insensitive neurons of the rat lingual trigeminal nerve.
(+)-3f is a promising drug candidate for the control of severe to moderate pain states with negligible effects on blood pressure.

3-methyl-5-hydroxy-5-trichloromethyl-1H-1-pyrazolcarboxyamide induces antinociception in the neurogenic and inflammatory phases of the formalin test in mice, and this effect does not involve opioid receptors.
(+)-3f administered at 3 mg/kg orally produces significant analgesic efficacy comparable to Gabapentin (100 mg/kg, po) in a rat formalin-induced pain model.
The 2-cyclohexylthiopyridine analogue (34S) exhibits partial agonism at hTRPV1 when administered orally at 10 mg/kg in the CCI neuropathic mouse model.
Regioisomeric 2-phenyl thiazole analogs display a striking disparity in COX-1 and COX-2 inhibitory activity.
N-(5-(1-(4-isobutyl-phenyl)-ethyl)-1,3,4-thiadiazol-2-yl)-acetamide exhibits the highest analgesic activity among the tested compounds with 69.8% in vitro.
BRACO19 demonstrates anti-HIV-1 activity by strengthening viral genomic stability.
Michellamine F prevents the cytopathic effect of HIV in the CEM-SS cell line.
Integramycin is a complex natural product with significant and novel activity against HIV-1 integrase, making it a promising candidate for new anti-HIV therapy.
IND02-trimer inhibits HIV-1 infection in MAGI cells with an EC50 in the low μM range.
IND02-trimer shows anti-viral activity against HIV-1 in peripheral blood mononuclear cells with an ED50 of 1–7 μM across clinical isolates from different HIV-1 clades.
BNM-III-170 (CD4mc) synergizes with a gp120-elicited immune response to protect rhesus macaques from high-dose intrarectal infection with a heterologous Clade C transmitted/founder SHIV.
Integramycin inhibits HIV-1 integrase-mediated coupled and strand transfer reactions with IC50 values of 3 µM and 4 µM, respectively.
RPR 103611 blocks HIV-1 infection at a post-binding, envelope-dependent step by interfering with the fusion of the virus with the cell membrane.
In vivo studies show that atazanavir exposure decreases substantially when intragastric pH is greater than 4.
3,16-di-O-acetylechinocystic acid (compound E121) exhibits potent anti-HIV activity with an EC50 of 2.3 pM and relatively low toxicity (IC50 13 pM).
Dibalanocarpol is a newly identified oligostilbene compound that exhibits modest HIV-inhibitory activity.
Genkwanol A is moderately active against HIV-1 in vitro.
Didemnaketal B inhibits HIV-1 protease with an IC50 of 10 µM.
Trigonolactone B shows significant inhibition against T20-resistant HIV-1 strains such as pNL4-3gp41(36G)V38E, N42S, and pNL4-3gp41(36G)V38A, N42T.
Thiamine disulfide is classified as a general inhibitor of Tat-activated transcription in HIV-1.
didemnaketal B exhibits potent HIV-1 protease inhibitory activity with an IC50 of 2–10 µM, likely through a dissociative mechanism.

BNM-III-170 directly inhibits the envelope (Env) proteins of HIV-1 viruses infecting SHIV-C5-infected monkeys, and neutralization of these viruses can be achieved using serum from the challenged monkeys after incubation with BNM-III-170.
BVDU administered orally or intravenously reduces viral titres by 95% and 93% respectively in immunocompetent outbred mice after three days of treatment.

Trigonolactone B is a daphnane diterpenoid with significant anti-HIV-1 activity, exhibiting EC50 values of 0.59–8.22 ng/mL and SI values of 1811–29,610 for HIV replication inhibition, and inhibiting HIV-1 fusion with EC50 values of 1.06–8.73 ng/mL, without inhibiting HIV-1 reverse transcriptase.
The in vivo distribution and intrahepatic deacylation of 3',5'-O-dipalmitoyl-5-fluoro-2'-deoxyuridine from liposomes depend strongly on liposome composition and drug to lipid ratio.
The intestinal permeability of 6-fluoroquinolone derivatives increases with lipophilicity up to a certain limit, after which permeability may decrease depending on the number of substituents.
After 28 days of dietary intake, dogs have decreased concentrations of 4-vinylguaiacol sulfate compared to cats.
In dogs, 5H-[1,3,4]thiadiazolo[3,2-a]pyrimidin-5-one demonstrates rapid oral absorption (Tmax 0.5 hr), high oral bioavailability (~92%), and rapid elimination (t1/2 2 hr).
In dogs, the rate of tubular transport for 3-aminohippurate is identical to that of 3-nitrohippurate, 4-nitrohippurate, and 4-aminohippurate.
Nitrofuryltriazole is a compound whose oral absorption and urinary excretion have been tested in dogs, with total urinary excretion measured over 0–6 hours after a single 5 mg/kg oral dose.
Methoxyimino cephem derivatives exhibit good oral absorbability, attributed to the N,IV-dimethylcarbamoyloxy group at the C-3 position.

Total body retention of [14C]pyridoxine administered orally is modestly but significantly lower in old rats compared to young rats, with similar tissue distribution and biokinetic parameters in both age groups.

1,2,3-triazolylmethylene derivatives of 6-(substituted methylene)penem are potent inhibitors of bacterial beta-lactamases and were selected for further evaluation based on their stability in human kidney homogenate, binding to human serum, and activity in a mouse infection model.
A key 5-(1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-1,2,4-oxadiazole compound demonstrates a favorable pharmacokinetic profile in rats after intravenous and oral administration.
2,3-dimethylindole-l-acetamide elevates gastric fluid pH upon oral administration in rats.
EACA exhibits significant species variation in therapeutic plasma concentrations, with nearly 100-fold differences observed between horses and dogs.
Cephalosporins bearing 2-amino-1-(3-methylamino-propyl)-1H-imidazo[4,5-b]pyridinium as the C-3' side chain exhibit high stability against AmpC beta-lactamase in vivo.

The (R)-morpholine-2-propionic acid group at the C6 position of the dihydropyrimidine core ring confers very weak CYP3A4 induction, even at 10 μM concentration, and increases systemic exposure and decreases clearance in rats and beagle dogs.
2-methoxy-1,3-thiazole 10d displays high clearance and low bioavailability in rats.
The differential excretion of diiodosalicylic acid by the kidney is associated with differences in its metabolic utilization.
Amylbiguanide demonstrates hypoglycemic activity when given orally or parenterally in diabetic humans and multiple species of normal and alloxan diabetic animals.
6-(aminomethyl)carbapenem possesses more potent in vitro antibacterial activity than its 6-(1'(R)-aminoethyl) analogue, but is inactive in vivo due to reduced stability.
2-(3-mercaptopropyl)pentanedioic acid is orally bioavailable in rats and exhibits efficacy in an animal model of neuropathic pain following oral administration.
Oral administration of Copper(II) nitrobenzene-2-oxa-1,4-diazole complexes profoundly affects the rate of absorption and assimilation of copper into various organs and tissues.

The therapeutic drug monitoring and pharmacokinetic studies of ornidazole are important due to drug interactions, toxicity, and the need to establish a permissible limit of imidazole drugs in human serum (10 mg/mL).
4-Hydroxypentenoyl rhodanine demonstrated high tolerability at relatively high concentrations in a phase I clinical trial in children with neuroblastoma.
Ferroptocide exhibits immunostimulatory activity in a murine cancer model.
Belinostat, a clinically relevant HDAC inhibitor, synergistically induces CLL cell death when coadministered with bortezomib.
5-diazo-4-oxo-L-norvaline (DON) is an inhibitor of residual L-asparaginase enzymatic activity in whole blood samples.
Pivalyloxymethyl butyrate increases survival in mice bearing Lewis lung carcinoma.
The cytotoxic effects of Belinostat correlate with histone H4 acetylation.
Hydroxyprolyl-hydroxyproline shows a remarkable positive correlation with the gut bacteria Collinsella and Coriobacteriaceae in AML patients.
4-deoxy-4-fluoro-xyloside derivatives act as nanomolar to micromolar inhibitors of U251 and U87 glioma cell proliferation.
Coumarin-thiophosphate hybrids exhibit anticancer activity, though with lower potency compared to reference compounds.
methyl-2-amino-5-((4-isopropylphenylthio)methyl)thiophene-3-carboxylate is a potent and selective anti-T-lymphoma/leukemia agent with an IC50 of 0.3–0.4 µM and a tumor selectivity (TS) of 100–144.
Cyclopropylmethyl pyrazole analogs have demonstrated inhibition of proliferation in an Ewing's Sarcoma cell line, outperforming earlier leads.
A pyrrolidine-carbamate based side-chain is considered unsuited for further development as a phenazine 5,10-dioxide anti-AML agent.
Galactose-coumarin derivatives can act as selective galectin-3 inhibitors and display anticancer efficacy comparable to nonselective galectin-1/galectin-3 inhibitors.
Hydroxyprolyl-Hydroxyproline is a fecal amino acid analog whose levels in AML patients show a remarkable positive correlation with the gut bacteria biomarkers Collinsella and Coriobacteriaceae.

9-(2-deoxy-beta-D-ribofuranosyl)-6-methylpurine (MeP-dR) is a purine nucleoside analog that, when selectively expressed in solid tumors via Escherichia coli PNP, leads to lasting tumor regression and cure.
N-lactoylcysteine may inhibit de novo pyrimidine synthesis in human leukaemia cells.
Erasin is a RAS-selective lethal compound that induces ferroptosis.

Methyl-2-amino-5-((4-isopropylphenylthio)methyl)thiophene-3-carboxylate is a potent and selective anti-T-lymphoma/leukemia agent with an IC50 of 0.3–0.4 µM and a tumor selectivity (TS) of 100–144, demonstrating strong preferential suppression of tumor cell lines compared to normal cells.
Administration of metal complexes of o-Hydroxydithiobenzoate reverses tumor growth and induces apoptosis in tumor-bearing mice.
Uncarinic acid C exhibits significant Th1 polarization effects and antiproliferative activity in HL-60 cells.

The hydrolysis of cholesterol carbonate can be catalyzed by imprinted polymer catalysts, resulting in a 27-fold enhancement over uncatalyzed reaction and a 2.4-fold improvement compared to control polymer.
Trimethylamine carboxyborane blocks regulator enzyme activities in the hepatic de novo synthesis of cholesterol.
(R)-3,5-dihydroxyheptanoate is the active side chain in statins that inhibits 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl coenzyme A reductase, a key enzyme in cholesterol biosynthesis.
3-N-(1',8'-Naphthalimido)propionic acid does not inhibit HMG CoA reductase, the regulatory enzyme of cholesterol synthesis.
3,4-dihydroxyhydrocinnamate lowers hepatic HMG-CoA reductase activity and hepatic cholesterol in rats fed a high cholesterol diet.
Statines are HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors that increase endothelial nitric oxide synthase (eNOS) expression and mRNA expression in human endothelial cells.
Ceestatin is a novel inhibitor of HCV replication that also inhibits HMG-CoA synthase.

5j (sodium (3R,5R)-7-(3-(N,N-dimethylsulfamoyl)-5-(4-fluorophenyl)-2-isopropyl-4phenyl-1H-pyrrol-1-yl)-3,5-dihydroxyheptanoate) is a 4-sulfamoyl pyrrole statin derivative that inhibits HMGCR with an affinity value of -12.17 kcal/mol and binding energy of -94.10 kcal/mol via five hydrogen bonds.
Among the oxygenated sterols in LDL, 4␤-OH-Chol is not among those that show high potency in lowering HMG-CoA reductase activity.
1-(4-Pyrimidinyl)-4-(1-(4-bromophenylsulfonyl)piperazin-4-ylcarbonyl)piperidine is an inhibitor of cholesterol biosynthesis, as demonstrated by its effect on hepatic lipid profiles in rats.
Ezetimibe selectively inhibits the intestinal absorption of cholesterol-related phytosterols.

(3R,5R)-7-(3-(N,N-dimethylsulfamoyl)-5-(4-fluorophenyl)-2-isopropyl-4-phenyl-1H-pyrrol-1-yl)-3,5dihydroxyheptanoate) binds to HMG-CoA reductase via strong hydrogen bonding interactions with the enzyme's 3,5,7-trihydroxy heptanoic acid fragment and its isopropyl group fitting into a small lipophilic pocket.
trans-1,4-bis[[1-cyclohexyl-3-(4-dimethylamino phenyl) ureido]methyl]cyclohexane (NTE-122) is a novel acyl-CoA:cholesterol acyltransferase (ACAT) inhibitor.
Phenothiazine-morpholine derivatives have been synthesized and evaluated for their effects on mechanisms involved in atherosclerosis, such as hyperlipidemia, oxidative stress, and inflammation.

Allyl[6-[4-(4-bromophenyl)-2H-chromen-7-yloxy]hexyl]methylamine inhibits cholesterol synthesis in primary rat hepatocytes, with its efficacy and potency determined by measuring residual inhibition after 2, 8, or 24 h incubation.

3 β -(2 -hydroxyethoxy)-5 α -cholest-8( 14)-en-15-one inhibits cholesterol biosynthesis in Hep G2 cells only weakly due to rapid degradation of its side chain and formation of 3 β -(2 -hydroxyethoxy)-15-keto-5 α -chol-8( 14)-en-24-oic acid, which is secreted into the culture medium.
The conversion of 3H-squalene to sterols by rat liver microsomes and cytosol is inhibited by individual rat and human plasma lipoproteins at various concentrations.
Benzophenone bis-sulfamate derivatives are potent steroid sulfatase inhibitors with submicromolar IC50 values (e.g., 2.0 nM for 29f) against recombinant, purified steroid sulfatase.

Trimethylamine carboxyborane suppresses the activity of key regulator enzymes of de novo lipid synthesis, accelerates fecal excretion of cholesterol via the biliary route, and modulates cholesterol content of lipoproteins to reduce VLDL and LDL cholesterol and elevate HDL cholesterol in rats.
Picolinoylpyrrolidine-2-carboxylic acid serves as a moiety in the design of DGAT-1 inhibitors targeting hyperlipidemia and metabolic disorders.
8-C-(E-phenylethenyl)quercetin inhibits proliferation and induces G2 phase arrest in human colon cancer cells.
THZ1 is the first inhibitor demonstrated to target a cysteine located outside the kinase domain of a kinase, providing a novel mechanism for covalent selectivity.
Diquas selectively targets mitochondria in NB4 cells.
Avitinib maleate is a human EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor that did not inhibit Giardia lamblia CBG99 growth above 50% at 5 µM in screening assays.
7-(2,3-epoxypropoxy)actinomycin D retains DNA binding property and enhances in vivo tumor-inhibiting activity compared to AMD.I0.
nogalamycin N-oxide (NSC116555) is a potent EGFR-tyrosine kinase inhibitor with an IC50 of 37.71 nM against EGFR-TK.
Compounds related to (3b,12b,14b,16b)-22,23-epoxy-3,12,14,16-tetrahydroxyergosta-5,7-dien-11-one induce dynAP-mediated Golgi fragmentation and apoptosis in human cancer cells.
Braftide, in combination with vemurafenib or other ATP-competitive inhibitors, acts synergistically to diminish paradoxical MAPK activation and sufficiently inhibit MAPK signaling in HEK293 cells.
7-(2,3-epoxypropoxy)actinomycin D retains DNA binding property and enhances in vivo tumor-inhibiting activity compared to actinomycin D analogues.
4-[(3,5-diamino-1H-pyrazol-4-yl)diazenyl]phenol inhibits proliferation of several cancer cell lines by blocking CDK2-cyclin E activity.

2'-deoxy-2'-methylene guanosine (MdGuo) inhibits the growth of mouse and human tumor cell lines in culture and is phosphorylated by kinases distinct from those activating 2'-deoxy-2'-methylene adenosine.

Inhibition of HDAC6 by N-hydroxy-N'-{4-[(6-methoxyquinolin-8-yl)amino]pentyl}pentanediamide leads to increased acetylation of alpha-tubulin and impairs cytoskeletal organization in glioblastoma cells.
Landomycin A inhibits cell cycle progression.
Periodate-oxidized 3-aminopyridine-adenine dinucleotide phosphate inhibits proliferation of human cancer cell lines in a dose-dependent manner.
EX527 potentiates the sensitivity of CD44+ HCT-15 cells to the Hsp90 inhibitor 17-AAG in human colon cancer cell lines.
Hyperosmotic sorbital stimulates aggregation of epidermal growth factor receptors.
The antiproliferative and pro-apoptotic potency of 4-[(3,5-diamino-1H-pyrazol-4-yl)diazenyl]phenol may result from inhibition of multiple kinases, including CDK2, CDK7, and CDK9.
Spinasaponin A methyl ester activates ERK1/2 MAPK signaling in HCT-116 colon cancer cells.
Bafilomycin F is a vacuolar-type Hϩ-ATPase inhibitor that blocks endosomal recycling and inhibits MAPK activation downstream of EGFR and GPCRs.
Braftide allosterically inhibits BRAF activation by disrupting BRAF homodimer formation, as shown by decreased BRAF homodimer formation at 50 µM in HEK293 cell lysates.
L-LSD does not significantly affect serotonin-stimulated adenylate cyclase activity in membranes from Helix and Aplysia neuronal cell bodies at concentrations around 10^-6 M.
2,7-bis-(4-amidinobenzylidene)-cycloheptan-1-one dihydrochloride (tPA stop) reduces NMDA-mediated excitotoxic neuronal death in mouse cortical cell cultures.
The effect of 4-Dimethylaminomethyl-1-(3-hydroxyphenyl)-1-nonen 3-one hydrochloride on mitochondrial respiration varies with the pH of the medium.
l-lysergic acid diethylamide is ineffective as a serotonin blocking agent in rat collicular adenylate cyclase assays.
4-Dimethylaminomethyl-1-(3-hydroxyphenyl)-1-nonen 3-one hydrochloride inhibits mitochondrial respiration in the presence of glutamate over the tested dose range.
6-amino-4-(4-phenoxyphenylethylamino)quinazoline (APEQ) induces the formation of active caspase-9 and caspase-3 in cerebellar neurons.
Citicoline treatment at 500 mg twice daily is associated with decreased self-reported mood states associated with cocaine craving in subjects with a history of cocaine dependence.

At concentrations higher than 5.0 µmoles, 4-Dimethylaminomethyl-1-(3-hydroxyphenyl)-1-nonen 3-one hydrochloride inhibits respiration in rat liver mitochondria when succinate and 3-hydroxybutyrate are used as substrates.
KB-R7943 demonstrates neuroprotection in models of stroke, ischemia, glutamate receptor overactivation, and hypoxia.
Encapsulation of diclofenac sodium within unilamellar liposomes of soya phosphatidylcholine significantly reduces myotoxicity after intramuscular administration.
Doxantrazole is a mast cell stabiliser that suppresses challenge-induced colonic secretion in cow's milk sensitised guinea pigs.
β-secretase inhibitor IV is a genuine BACE-1 inhibitor.
(3-[18F]fluoropropyl) triphenylphosphonium cation has lower uptake by myocardial tissue compared to longer-chain analogs.
L-Lysergic acid diethylamide, the inactive stereoisomer of serotonin, does not block serotonin-stimulated adenylate cyclase activity in Helix and Aplysia ganglia membranes.
FeTMPyP decreases apoptosis without affecting nitric oxide (NO) production in a model of myocardial ischemia/reperfusion injury.
2,7-bis-(4-amidinobenzylidene)-cycloheptan-1-one dihydrochloride (tPA stop) prevents tPA-dependent potentiation of NMDA receptor-evoked calcium influx.
Aprosulate sodium inhibits thrombus formation in an arterio-venous shunt model in a dose-dependent manner.
Radiolabeled N-benzyl-isatin sulfonamide analogues are potential radiotracers for imaging caspase-3 activity in vivo.

F-18 labeled Fluorobenzyltriphenylphosphonium (F-18 FBnTP) PET tracer provides stable delineation of the ischemic area and shows no washout or redistribution during 2-minute episodes of ischemia in a rat model, supporting its potential for clinical applications including tracer injection and exercise stress outside the scanner.

Incorporation of three fluorine atoms into the thioamidine heterocyclic core of γ-secretase modulator 4 reduced the pKa of the methyl substituent from 7.7 to 7.0, increased the hERG IC50 from 2.1 μM to 10.1 μM, and increased logD by 0.3, while maintaining high metabolic stability (HLM CL int,app < 8 mL/min/kg).
Simvastatin sodium salt is synthesized by the acylation of monacolin J sodium salt (MJSS) catalyzed by LovD, using DMB-S-MMP as the acyl donor.
Cholesterol sulfate synthesis in NHBE cells increases in parallel with enhanced cross-linked envelope formation upon PMA treatment.
Cholesterol [35S]sulfate is formed by the transfer of [35S]sulfate from 3'-phosphoadenosine 5'-phospho[35S]sulfate to cholesterol by sulfotransferases in cell extracts.
Aurofusarin negatively affects the accumulation of carotenoids and vitamin E in quail.
Red blood cell cholinesterase inhibition levels higher than 40% with Eptastigmine are associated with subjective discomfort and cholinergic adverse events.
bis[(3aRS,4SR,7aRS)-4-(3,4-dimethoxy-(Z)-styryl)-hexahydro-4-indolinol]-maleate (34-250), a hypocholesterolemic drug, was cataractogenic in animal models.
5j demonstrates favorable ADME and toxicity profiles and is considered a potent hepatoselective pyrrole drug candidate for cholesterol and Alzheimer's disease treatment.

Cholesterol stearate is completely hydrolyzed to produce the TMS ether of cholesterol and the n-butyl ester of stearic acid when treated with n-butyl ester derivatization with hydrochloric acid at elevated temperatures.
(1R,3β)-3-hydroxycholestane-4,6-diene-1,25-diol diacetate can be synthesized from a 5,7-diene precursor via kinetic control of equilibration and elimination reactions.
(24E)-3α,7α,12α-trihydroxy-5β-cholest-24-enoyl-CoA is an intermediate in bile acid synthesis that can be hydrated by recombinant (R)-hydratase.
Aurofusarin supplementation at 26•4 mg/kg feed in Japanese quail diets has pronounced negative effects on egg quality and immunity.
Polymyxin B is associated with increased nephrotoxicity when administered to patients with chronic renal disease, even when renal function is normal prior to drug administration.
Ferrocenoyl 17βhydroxy-estra-1,3,5(10)-trien-3-olate exhibits greater cytotoxicity than ferrocene and ferrocenium in HT-29 colon cancer cells.
3,24-bis(3-bromophenoxy)cholane undergoes palladium-catalyzed amination with polyamines and polyoxadiamines to form macrocycles containing steroidal and polyamine moieties, yielding 38–65%.
The biosynthesis of zorbamycin is affected by three different genetic loci located on the chromosome.
Palmityl oleate is synthesized in a dose- and time-dependent manner by LPL from oleic acid and hexadecanol.
3 alpha-benzoyloxy-5 alpha-cholest-8(14)-en-15-one is a C27 15-oxygenated sterol derivative synthesized for evaluation of its inhibitory effects on sterol biosynthesis in animal cells in culture.
The preliminary safety profile of 4-(4-(1-(2-(5-chlorothiophen-2-yl)acetyl)-6-methoxy-4oxo-1,2-dihydroquinazolin-3(4H)-yl)phenyl)morpholin-3-one is comparable to that of betrixaban.
Hexobromocyclododecane, a flame retardant, enhances high-fat diet-induced obesity, hepatic steatosis, and impairs glucose and lipid homeostasis in mice.

Lysine-cholesterol is an alkaline amino acid-based cationic lipid that, when used to form cationic lipoplexes, provides high serum resistance and low toxicity in transfection of 293T, HeLa, and NIH3T3 cell lines.

1,2,3-triazolylmethylene derivatives of 6-(substituted methylene)penem are potent inhibitors of bacterial beta-lactamases and were selected for further evaluation based on their stability in human kidney homogenate, binding to human serum, and activity in a mouse infection model.
[1-13C]2-deoxyglucose can be used to measure blood lactate labeling in mice via primed infusion, enabling assessment of metabolic labeling in vivo.
The conversion of Hydroquinone-glutathione to a di-glutathione conjugate in hepatocytes requires rate constants that are optimized to account for mass balance in metabolic studies.
17-[18F]fluoroheptadecanoic acid has a different elimination half-time than 16-[18F]fluorohexadecanoic acid in NMRI mice.

Liposomes containing 2-(4-((1,5-bis(octadecenoyl)1,5-dioxopentan-2-yl)carbamoyl) pyridin-1-ium-1-yl)acetate (DCPA-H2O) become protonated below pH 6.8, enabling fast self-targeting to infectious biofilms after tail-vein injection in mice.
In rats, the rate of transport of [14C]-D-lactate into liver in vivo is 1.8 µmol·min⁻¹·g⁻¹, with a volume of distribution of 0.71 ± 0.07 mL/g and an extraction of 91% ± 9% for unidirectional influx.
Serum samples were collected at 0.083 hours and 2 hours after DSF administration in the serum bactericidal assay.
Strasseriolide A is a compound evaluated for in vivo toxicity and pharmacokinetic parameters in mice following intravenous administration.
C-5 amide analogues of thiomorpholine S,S-dioxide demonstrate good in vitro potency against gram-positive bacteria.
1-(4-chlorophenyl)-4,4,4-trifluoro-3-hydroxy-2-buten-1-one enhances antibacterial activity at acidic pH by lowering bacterial intracellular pH.
The liver plays a significant role in the clearance of 2,4-dinitrophenyl-N-acetylcysteine (DNP-NAC) from the bloodstream.
Bisphosphonate-carbamate conjugates are designed to deliver antibiotics to bone tissue by leveraging the bone-targeting properties of bisphosphonates.
19F NMR chemical shifts of metabolites in mice after fluoroacetamide administration are consistent with fluoroacetohydroxamic acid in the liver.
[14C] acetanilide is a precursor of acetaminophen in rat liver, and its hepatic intrinsic clearance influences the extent of acetaminophen sulfation in the liver.
Introduction of the 3-aminomethylazetidine moiety at position 2 in the methylpiperazine derivative scaffold increases stability in mouse liver microsomes.

Tetrafluorophenoxy methyl ketone analogues exhibit dose-dependent reduction of liver injury endpoints in an R-Fas-mediated model, with ED50 values in the mg/kg range depending on administration route.

1-(4-chlorophenyl)-4,4,4-trifluoro-3-hydroxy-2-buten-1-one is a protonophore with potent in vitro and in vivo antibacterial activity against methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA), showing a MIC of 4 µg/ml and reduced intracellular MRSA in macrophages.
Chloroaminothiazole is a chemical group that, when incorporated into a siderophore-conjugated g-lactam antibiotic analog, favorably modulates several biological properties.
MeAIB is not an inhibitor of System N in hepatocytes.
Amidox is a novel anticancer drug that undergoes extensive metabolism in rat liver, with about 55% of applied Amidox accumulating as metabolites in the effluent perfusate after liver perfusion.
N-Benzylacetyl-d-alanylthioacetic acid is a competitive inhibitor of metallo-beta-lactamase L1 from Stenotrophomonas maltophilia with a K(i) of 1.6 ± 0.3 µM.
N-Benzylacetyl-D-alanylthioacetic acid acts as an irreversible inhibitor of ␤-lactamase II from B. cereus by covalently modifying the enzyme's metal binding cysteine ligand.
Upon incubation with N-Benzylacetyl-d-alanylthioacetic acid, 2 mol of mercaptoacetate covalently bind to metallo-beta-lactamase L1, but the covalently modified enzyme retains full activity.
4-(2-Sulfoethyl)phenylmethanesulfonyl chloride exhibits distinct inhibitory effects on Penicillin amidase.
Naphthylfuranones act as inhibitors of multiple Mur enzymes, with structure-activity relationships favoring C3 b and C5 a substitution patterns for broad-spectrum enzyme inhibition.
3-(heptyloxy)-N-hydroxybenzamide, a monohydroxamic acid-containing compound, does not inhibit the metallo-beta-lactamase Bla2.
Substituents at positions 5 and 7 of the 3-phenyl-4-hydroxycoumarin ring influence its inhibitory potency against HIV-1 protease.
4-(3-aminophenyl)pyridine-2,6-dicarboxylic acid forms a ternary complex with VIM-2 metallo-beta-lactamase, as demonstrated by UV-Vis spectroscopy and native-state ESI-MS.
7-(3-sulfenylazetidin-1-yl)fluoroquinolone carboxylic acid derivatives (compounds 9a-f) exhibit moderate antibacterial activity against Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria.

Benzimidazolylbenzoylhydrazine, when used as a moiety on N-1 of β-lactams with phenyl, 4-chlorophenyl, or 2,4-dichlorophenyl substituents, inhibits M. tuberculosis H37Rv by 97% at a dose level of 12 µ g/mL in vitro.

7-phenylacetamido-3-chloro-3-cephem-4-carboxylic acid displays P-lactam reactivity that is 30–80% greater than 7-aminocephalosporanic acids and 12–13 times greater than 7-amino-3-chloro-3-cephem-4-carboxylic acids.
The inhibitory potency and mutant resistance spectrum of 3-benzenesulfonyl-1H-indole-2-carboxyamides depend on the number and position of substituents on the phenyl ring.
Gyramide A targets the DNA gyrase resistance-determining region adjacent to the DNA cleavage gate, representing a new site for inhibitor design.
Isoxazolyldichlorocatechol, when introduced as a C-7 substituent in catecholylcephalosporin compounds, enhances antimicrobial activity more effectively than substitution at the C-3 position.
4-(2-sulfoethyl)phenylmethanesulfonyl chloride is a weaker inactivator of penicillin amidase compared to phenylmethanesulfonyl chloride.
3-acetylphenyloxazolidinone is a chemical moiety whose key interactions can be mimicked by 3-substituted phenyloxazolidinones to explore structure-activity relationships.
Benzyl substitution at position 6 in 3-chloro-9-methylpyridazino[3,4-b][1,5]benzoxazepin-5(6H)-ones is associated with higher antituberculotic activity than shorter alkyl chains.
Indolin-2-one nitroimidazole exhibits potent activity against aerobic bacteria, indicating a novel mode of action.
The K i value of diformylaminomethylbenzo[b]thiophene against AmpC β-lactamase is 30–43 nM, similar to the lead compound and other related derivatives.
The glycyl moiety of Restricticin is essential for its antifungal activity.
Molnupiravir probably reduces hospital admission in patients with non-severe covid-19.
Replacing the (4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)-methyl group with 4-methylpiperidinyl in the analog structure leads to an 8-fold decrease in anti-HCV potency.

methyl 4-(2-chloro-4-fluorophenyl)-6-methyl-2-(pyridin-2-yl)-1,4-dihydropyrimidine-5-carboxylate (HAP-1) is a heteroaryldihydropyrimidine antiviral that inhibits hepatitis B virus (HBV) production by enhancing the rate and extent of capsid protein assembly in vitro.
Both low and high concentrations of HAP-1 reduce HBV virion production by affecting capsid assembly.
Sorbital, as an activating salt, modulates the inhibition profile of HRV 3C protease by classic and specifically designed inhibitors.
Intravenous arginine-ascorbate treatment can result in undetectable hepatitis C viral loads 5 years after therapy in patients who were not cleared after two treatments with ribavirin and interferon.
Imiquimod exerts antiviral activity by stimulating the production of interferon-α and other cytokines.

Arginine-ascorbate exhibits synergistic antiviral action by enhancing the antiviral response via interferons and inducing the expression of over 850 novel host cell genes, which is sufficient to completely block replication and spread of myxoma virus in human fibroblasts and to inhibit the spread of vaccinia virus and tanapox virus to neighboring cells.
Acetylation of the 1H-1,2,4-triazol-3-ol ring dramatically reduces the potency of the analogues in the series.

6-styryl-pyran-2-one derivatives have been evaluated for anti-tuberculosis, anti-parasitic, and cytotoxic activities, including inhibition of Mycobacterium tuberculosis, Trypanosoma brucei rhodesiense, T. cruzi, Leishmania donovani, and Plasmodium falciparum.
HAP-1 is an allosteric effector that enhances the rate and extent of HBV capsid assembly at low concentrations by favoring an assembly-active state in vitro.

Dihydroethanoanthracenic derivatives can enhance the activity of chloroquine against Plasmodium falciparum chloroquine-resistant parasites by acting on the rate of chloroquine accumulation and its access to ferriprotoporphyrin IX, potentially through competition at efflux translocation sites.
Citronellyl 6-(dimethylamino)hexanoate enhances the delivery of cidofovir through and into human skin.
Benzo[c]phenanthridinones are pharmaceutically interesting molecules for which new synthetic methods have been developed.
4-pyridylquinoline derivatives can be synthesized via palladium-catalyzed cross-coupling of 6-bromo-4-chloroquinoline intermediates.
6-amino-4-anilinoquinazoline compounds can be synthesized using a general synthetic approach and serve as intermediates for further derivatization.
Imidazo[1,2-c]quinazoline derivatives are useful in pharmaceutical applications for their antimicrobial, antitumor, and antipsychotic activities.
1,3-diaminopyrrolidin-2-one derivatives can be synthesized via a route involving oxazolidinone intermediates and potassium enolate chemistry.
3-Phosphinoylindole derivatives are important pharmaceutical drugs and ligands.
3-Phosphinoylindole derivatives can be synthesized via a one-step radical cascade cycloaddition-desulfonylation process.

Imidazo[1,2-c]quinazoline derivatives can be synthesized efficiently via a copper-catalyzed one-pot tandem reaction involving Ullmann-type C-N coupling and intramolecular dehydrative cyclization of substituted 2-(2-bromophenyl)-1H-imidazoles and formamide.

4,5-dihydrobenzo[g]indazoles can be synthesized in excellent yield and with high regioselectivity from 2-fluorovinylcarbonyl acyldihydronaphthalenes using bifunctional nucleophiles such as hydrazines and amidines.
Dioxopyrido[1,2-a]indole compounds have been synthesized as part of a flexible synthetic approach to mitosene-type analogues with varied ring sizes and substituents.
Indole azaborines can be synthesized using a strategy that involves activation of organotrifluoroborate precursors with a fluorophile and reaction with o-aminostyrene derivatives.
Benzoindoloquinoxaline derivatives have been synthesized and assessed for anti-VSV activity, interferon-inducing ability, and cytotoxicity.
The benzo[a]pyrrolo[c]carbazole core system is a fused carbazole derivative that can be synthesized via BF3-mediated cyclization in dichloromethane at -40°C.
β-(2-bromophenyl)acrylamide serves as a precursor for the synthesis of 4-arylquinolin-2(1H)-ones via sequential Heck reaction and Cu-catalyzed cyclization.
4-pyridazone derivatives have been synthesized and tested for chemotherapeutic properties, including activity against tuberculosis.
Imidazo[1,2-b][1,2,4,5]tetrazine 1a-d serves as a starting material for the synthesis of various compounds in the presence of thiol and triethylamine.
2,4-dimethoxy-tetrahydropyrimido[4,5-b]quinolin-6(7H)-one (2,4-dimethoxy-THPQs) can be synthesized via acetic acid-mediated multicomponent synthesis.
Iodo-substituted indenopyridin-2-ones can be synthesized from 4-substituted 6-benzyl 3,6-dihydropyridones using NIS as an iodinating agent.
Ethynylindole can be synthesized in three steps from 5-chloroindole, utilizing iodine installation at the 2-position, Sonogashira coupling with (trimethylsilyl)acetylene, and deprotection.
T3P functions as a coupling and dehydrating agent in the one-pot sequential cascade synthesis of 2-trifluoromethylquinazolin-4(3H)-ones.
Nexturastat A is a selective HDAC6 inhibitor that, in combination with PD-1 immune blockade, significantly improves anti-tumor immune responses and reduces tumor growth in melanoma models.
1 5-Scysteinyldopa serves as a specific metabolic marker for tumor progression and early detection of occult metastases in patients with malignant melanoma.
Pemrametostat is a PRMT5 inhibitor that enhances the efficacy of Palbociclib in CDK-i resistant melanoma cells and delays the onset of CDK-i resistance in naive cells.
SCH-527123 inhibits proliferation, migration, and invasion, and enhances apoptosis in melanoma cancer cell lines.
Nexturastat A inhibits the growth of B16 melanoma cells and demonstrates antiproliferative effects.
Pivalyloxymethyl butyrate increases survival in mice bearing Lewis lung carcinoma.
Gold resveratrol hollow nanoparticles, when exposed to 808 nm laser, induce cell cycle arrest, apoptosis, and cell death in A375 melanoma cells.
MSeH reduces melanoma cell metastasis as a nutritional adjuvant by suppressing integrin expression and inhibiting MMPs.
The cytotoxic effects of Belinostat correlate with histone H4 acetylation.
N-(3,5-dimethylphenyl)-3-methoxybenzamide arrests melanocyte and melanoma cell proliferation in the G1 phase of the cell cycle and moderately suppresses tumor growth in vivo.
6-phosphoguanosine diphosphate (6PGD) expression and activity are upregulated in renal cell carcinoma (RCC) patients and cells, contributing to tumorigenesis and resistance.
Belinostat, a clinically relevant HDAC inhibitor, synergistically induces CLL cell death when coadministered with bortezomib.
In the B16/C3 in vitro melanoma cell model, para-iodobenzamide derivatives show higher cellular uptake compared to other positional isomers.
Resveratrol suppresses T-cell expansion and pro-inflammatory cytokine production in vivo and in vitro.
SCH-527123 reduces tumor cell proliferation, angiogenesis, and enhances apoptosis in human colorectal cancer liver metastases.
Galactose-coumarin derivatives can act as selective galectin-3 inhibitors and display anticancer efficacy comparable to nonselective galectin-1/galectin-3 inhibitors.
2',5'-dimethoxychalcone derivatives exhibit low micromolar in vitro activity against several human cancer cell lines.
(-)-3-hydroxy-2-methylbutyrolactone increases melanin production and tyrosinase activity in B16 melanoma cells.
Mepitiostane administration for approximately 6 months in meningioma patients may not produce a marked change in tumor size.
Selected 1-(imidazol-4-ylmethyl)-1,4-benzodiazepine analogues demonstrate in vivo antitumor activity in a Rat-1 intraperitoneal/intraperitoneal tumor model.
rsCD4 inhibits HIV-I replication synergistically when combined with AZT and rIFN-a-A in both PBMC and CD4-positive T cell lines.
BRACO19 demonstrates anti-HIV-1 activity by strengthening viral genomic stability.
Michellamine F prevents the cytopathic effect of HIV in the CEM-SS cell line.
Integramycin is a complex natural product with significant and novel activity against HIV-1 integrase, making it a promising candidate for new anti-HIV therapy.
ddA can be incorporated as a moiety in di-aryl nucleoside phosphotriesters designed as potential anti-HIV-1 pronucleotides.
BNM-III-170 (CD4mc) synergizes with a gp120-elicited immune response to protect rhesus macaques from high-dose intrarectal infection with a heterologous Clade C transmitted/founder SHIV.
Transitmycin is active against HIV subtypes B and C.
Tetradecylthiopropionic-CoA facilitates translocation of enzymes from cytosol to microsomes in rat liver cell-free extract in vitro.
Transitmycin is virucidal against recombinant Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) but is non-cytotoxic to mouse embryo fibroblast cell lines.
IND02-trimer shows anti-viral activity against HIV-1 in peripheral blood mononuclear cells with an ED50 of 1–7 μM across clinical isolates from different HIV-1 clades.
Atazanavir is a potent once-daily protease inhibitor used for the treatment of HIV type I infection, effective in both treatment-experienced and treatment-naive patients.
Neochamaejasmine A increases the bioavailability of Chamaechromone in rats by inhibiting breast cancer resistance protein (BCRP) and multidrug resistance protein 2 (MRP2)-mediated efflux.
Penicillixanthone A acts as a dual-coreceptor antagonist targeting both CCR5 and CXCR4, representing a new type of potential lead product for anti-HIV therapeutic development.
Penicillixanthone A exhibits potent anti-HIV-1 activity by inhibiting infection of CCR5-tropic HIV-1 SF162 and CXCR4-tropic HIV-1 NL4-3, with IC50 values of 0.36 µM and 0.26 µM, respectively.

Stilbenavir (NSC34931) is a stilbene disulfonic acid compound that inhibits the strand transfer (ST) step of HIV-1 integrase by a novel mechanism, showing cytopathic effects on HIV-1 and sub-micromolar potency against clinically relevant drug-resistant mutants.
Nelfinavir is used as part of combination antiretroviral therapy in patients with HIV and tuberculosis.
Bentysrepinine (Y101) demonstrates low drug resistance and sustained virological response even after treatment discontinuation.
3,16-di-O-acetylechinocystic acid (compound E121) exhibits potent anti-HIV activity with an EC50 of 2.3 pM and relatively low toxicity (IC50 13 pM).
5-(1,2-diiodo)vinyluridine has a selectivity index (SI) of 1.5 against HCV replication in the replicon system.
NADPH (reduced form of nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide phosphate) is used in an NADPH-regenerating system to provide reducing equivalents for in vitro CYP3A1 and CYP3A2 enzyme assays.
(2S,3R)-Hydroxycitric acid reduces carbohydrate digestion in a human cell model system.
3,4-dihydroxyhydrocinnamate lowers hepatic HMG-CoA reductase activity and hepatic cholesterol in rats fed a high cholesterol diet.
Andrographolide and its derivatives have attracted wide attention for their anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative activities.
3,4-dihydroxyhydrocinnamate is effective in improving lipid metabolism by increasing the plasma HDL-C/Total-C ratio and decreasing the atherogenic index.
(2S,3R)-Hydroxycitric acid inhibits pancreatic α-amylase and small intestine α-glucosidase.
(R)-5-hydroxy-1,7-diphenyl-3-heptanone improves oxidative stress states in T2DM mice.
3,3′,4,5,5′-pentahydroxystilbene exhibits high lipid oxidation activity in lard models.
Cinnamonmethylhydroxychalcone (MHCP) extracts possess hypoglycemic properties by activating the insulin receptor through multiple mechanisms and increasing glycogen synthase activity.
(R)-5-hydroxy-1,7-diphenyl-3-heptanone significantly reduces blood glucose levels in type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) mice induced by high-fat diet combined with STZ-nicotine.
Hydroxyfuroic acid compounds have been synthesized and tested for their ability to activate the human insulin receptor.
4',7,8-trihydroxyisoflavanone is a novel isoflavone metabolite identified in human urine after soy supplementation.
Hydroxyfuroic acid, derived from Demethylasterriquinone B1, retains the capability to activate the insulin receptor.
(2S,4S)-2,4-dihydroxyheptadec-16-ynyl acetate inhibits acetyl CoA carboxylase.
The rigid dioxaadamantane core is a structural feature present in some natural products and is associated with potent pharmaceutical activity.

5-Fluoro-N-(9,10-dihydro-9,10-dioxoanthracen-8-yl)-1Hindole-2-carboxamide derivatives improve lipid abnormalities such as hypertriglyceridemia and hypercholesterolemia, and increase HDL levels in Triton-induced rats.

(2R)-3-(3,4-dihydroxy phenyl)-2-[(Z)-3-(3,4-dihydroxy phenyl)acryloyl]oxy-propionic acid is identified as an anti-gastric-cancer ingredient with 2 key targets in network pharmacology analysis of Herba Sarcandrae.
Triptolide is a potent diterpenoid triepoxide whose medicinal properties can be optimized through synthetic organic strategies.
1-cyclohexyl-4-hydroxyaminobenzene (CyAm) induces oxidative stress and alters amino acid, energy, and lipid metabolism pathways in mice.
TTA is a hypolipidemic compound that stimulates adipose PEPCK, supporting its role in promoting fatty acid storage and glucose disposal.

Diaminoguanidinoacetic acid regioisomers induce weight loss in obese diabetic ob/ob mice, with a decrease in fat body mass twice that of lean body mass, indicating selective adipose tissue weight loss.
The 8-sulfophenylxanthine derivative PSB-603 is metabolically highly stable in human, rat, and mouse.
Introduction of the 3-aminomethylazetidine moiety at position 2 in the methylpiperazine derivative scaffold increases stability in mouse liver microsomes.
The thioacetate methyl ester prodrug derivative of compound 6m acts as an orally active and potent inhibitor of endothelin-converting enzyme-1 (ECE-1) activity in rats.
Mexrenoate potassium exhibits equivalent antihypertensive responses to spironolactone in dogs with established hypertension (Page model).
3,4-dihydroxyhydrocinnamate lowers hepatic HMG-CoA reductase activity and hepatic cholesterol in rats fed a high cholesterol diet.
1-(D-3-acetylthio-2-methylpropanoyl)-L-prolyl-L-phenylalanine is a novel orally active angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitor.
A key 5-(1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-1,2,4-oxadiazole compound demonstrates a favorable pharmacokinetic profile in rats after intravenous and oral administration.
The metabolism of TMN in rats requires the presence of both microsomal and soluble liver fractions.

The (R)-morpholine-2-propionic acid group at the C6 position of the dihydropyrimidine core ring confers very weak CYP3A4 induction, even at 10 μM concentration, and increases systemic exposure and decreases clearance in rats and beagle dogs.

[18F]1-Fluoro-2,4-dinitrobenzene, when administered to the perfused working rat heart, is subject to extraction, dehalogenation, and reversible incorporation into a tissue pool not involved in its detoxification.
Amidox is a novel anticancer drug that undergoes extensive metabolism in rat liver, with about 55% of applied Amidox accumulating as metabolites in the effluent perfusate after liver perfusion.
Quinoxalin-2-mercapto-acetyl-urea analogs display good stability in human liver microsomes and show little or no inhibition of P450 3A4.
Tofogliflozin improves glycemic control in diabetic rats and mice.
1-(4-Pyrimidinyl)-4-(1-(4-bromophenylsulfonyl)piperazin-4-ylcarbonyl)piperidine is an inhibitor of cholesterol biosynthesis, as demonstrated by its effect on hepatic lipid profiles in rats.
Carboxypropylsulphoxypropionic acid is a urinary metabolite of [1-14C]tetradecylthiopropionic acid in rats.
Prenalterol demonstrates a high degree of species specificity for O-sulfation over O-glucuronidation in dogs, similar to rodents.
HHMA (3,4-methylenedioxy-N-hydroxy-methylamphetamine) can be quantitatively measured in rat urine using LC-ESI-MS following solid-phase extraction.
2-methoxy-1,3-thiazole 10d displays high clearance and low bioavailability in rats.
Harmol glucuronidation in rat liver is relatively insensitive to hypoxia, but sensitivity increases markedly in fasted animals.
The conversion of Hydroquinone-glutathione to a di-glutathione conjugate in hepatocytes requires rate constants that are optimized to account for mass balance in metabolic studies.
Administration of metal complexes of o-Hydroxydithiobenzoate reverses tumor growth and induces apoptosis in tumor-bearing mice.

N-(2-bromo-5-(trifluoromethyl) phenyl)-6-methoxy-7-(3-(piperidin-1-yl)propoxy)quinazolin-4-amine (V1801) is a gefitinib analogue that induces apoptosis in L858R/T790M-EGFR harboring non-small cell lung cancer cells and overcomes gefitinib resistance in mice inoculated with NCI-H1975 cells.
N-acrylation of EF24 analogs bearing a 3,4-difluorobenzylidene moiety leads to a significant increase in anticancer activity against pancreatic carcinoma models.
The metal complexes of o-Hydroxydithiobenzoate inhibit tumor growth in vitro and prolong survival in tumor-bearing mice.
Pivalyloxymethyl butyrate increases survival in mice bearing Lewis lung carcinoma.
N-(4-(1,3,2-dithiarsinan-2-yl)phenyl)acrylamide (AAZ2) inhibits the growth of gastric cancer xenograft tumors in vivo.
Benzimidazo[2,1-a]isoquinoline carboxamide derivatives with the side chain attached to one of the terminal rings of the chromophore short axis show reasonable cytotoxicity in vitro and in vivo.
Tetrahydroquinoline-pyrimidine potently inhibits tumor growth in an A375 melanoma xenograft model.
APPCL is effective against ovarian cancer xenograft tumors and cancer cells resistant to standard-of-care drugs such as Taxol and cisplatin in an in vivo mouse model.
N-(3,5-dimethylphenyl)-3-methoxybenzamide arrests melanocyte and melanoma cell proliferation in the G1 phase of the cell cycle and moderately suppresses tumor growth in vivo.
Ethyl 4-oxo-2-phenyl-1,4-dihydroquinoline-6-carboxylate (AS1712) is a low-toxicity inhibitor of lung cancer cell proliferation and xenograft tumor growth.
Arylidine derivatives of 3-acetyl-1-aminoquinolin-2(1H)-one have been studied for their inhibitory effect on thymidylate synthase in the DMBA model of breast cancer using in silico prediction.

Prophylactic administration of 2-phenyl-5-phenylethynyl-1-benzofuran (2FF) delays or prevents the development of HER2+ luminal B and basal mammary tumors in mice when administered orally at doses of 20 mM and 50 mM.
Terephthalanilide derivatives have demonstrated anticancer activity against transplantable L1210 leukaemia in mice, with activity being a parabolic function of drug lipophilicity.
Selenobetaine methyl ester presents chemopreventive activity against chemically induced tumors in animal models.
18F-labeled RGD peptide generated by tetrazine-trans-cyclooctene ligation demonstrates prominent tumor uptake in vivo and confirmed receptor specificity.

Intratumoral injection of tetra-O-methyl nordihydroguaiaretic acid in a C3-cell induced C57bl/6 mouse tumor model results in substantial tumoricidal activity that correlates with reduced tumor cell CDC2 protein levels.
The genotoxicity of 2-Amino-1-methyl-6-phenylimidazo(4,5-b)pyridine N-oxide (Trp-P-2) in mice depends on the induction of CYP enzymes and phase II enzymes.
Intraperitoneal administration of salirasib inhibits tumor growth in A549 and HTB58 cell nude mouse models.

Dipyridylketone 4,4-dimethylthiosemicarbazone (Dp44mT) significantly reduces tumor weight to 47% of control in mice with chemotherapy-resistant lung carcinoma after 5 days of treatment, with no changes in animal weight or hematological indices, indicating selective tumor growth inhibition.

(S)-1-[N-(4-chlorobenzyl) succinamoyl] pyrrolidine-2-carbaldehyde, as a cholinesterase inhibitor, demonstrates protective activity against age-induced apoptosis in primary cultures of rat cerebral cortical and cerebellar granule cells.
CMTI decreases quinolinic acid-induced excitotoxic neuronal damage in rat cortical slices.
Bilobalide inhibits hypoxia-induced choline release from rat hippocampal slices.
Pyrazolo[3,4-b][1,8]naphthyridine derivatives are less potent cholinesterase inhibitors than tacrine, with IC50 values against total cholinesterase in rat brain tissue ranging from 6000 to 32200 nM.

Intraperitoneal administration of 4-acetamidophenylboronic acid pinacol ester to rats with orchiectomy-induced cognitive deficits ameliorates disrupted behavior and neuronal damage caused by hormonal deprivation.
Oxo-morpholino-adamantane increases membrane resistance in mature rat primary cortical neurons compared to immature neurons.
α-aminovalerophenone derivatives directly block dopamine uptake in rat brain synaptosomes and transfected HEK293 cells.
Hemicholinium mustard does not significantly affect synaptosomal uptake of dopamine and GABA at a dose that causes substantial HACU inhibition in rat cortical synaptosomes.

The (-)-enantiomer of 12-amino-6,7,10,11-tetrahydro-9-methyl-7,11-methanocycloocta[b]quinoline (9M-HTH) is approximately three times more active than tacrine in inhibiting acetylcholinesterase from bovine erythrocytes.
Alpha-fluoromethylhistidine depletes neural histamine and does not prevent caloric stimulation-induced inhibition of locus coeruleus neuronal activity in rats.

(S)-1-[N-(4-chlorobenzyl)succinamoyl]pyrrolidine-2-carbaldehyde (ONO-1603) protects against age-induced apoptosis in cultured rat cerebellar and cerebral neurons, with maximal protection at 0.03 µM and a protective range of 0.03 to 1 µM.
Benzo[b]pyrazolo[4,3-g][1,8]naphthyridine derivatives can act as inhibitors of Electrophorus electricus acetylcholinesterase (EeAChE), with some analogues showing high potency and selectivity.
Talaromycesone A is a new oxaphenalenone that inhibits acetylcholinesterase activity with an IC50 value in the range of 1.6 to 7.5 μM.
N-(methyl-carbamoyloxy)-N'-methylurea, a degradation product of caracemide, does not inhibit choline acetyltransferase from rat brain.
Administration of (m-nitrobenzoyl)-alanine increases kynurenic acid concentration in dialysates from gerbils with a dorsal hippocampal dialysis probe or rats with a parietal cortex dialysis probe.

N-((3-(4-chlorophenyl)-1-phenyl-1H-pyrazole-4-yl) methylene) benzenamine is a potent acetylcholinesterase (AChE) inhibitor with a pIC50 of 4.2 and acts as a highly selective inhibitor of goat liver monoamine oxidase B (MAO-B).
Dihydroxyphenyl acetate has no effect on THir neuron survival at concentrations up to 300 µM after 24 hours in primary rat mesencephalic cultures.
Tripropyl lead inhibits the oxidation of glucose by slices of brain cortex in vitro.
Zinc triethanolamine complex inhibits rat brain monoamine oxidase-B (MAO-B) in a time-dependent and reversible manner, with lower potency compared to cadmium triethanolamine complex.
AY-9944 treatment in rats is associated with ictal EEG activity in the hippocampus, a feature not observed in models for typical absence seizures.
2,3-diacyloxybenzoyl derivatives based on N-(aminobutyl)glycine exhibit the most potent antibacterial activity among the tested compounds against Gram-negative bacteria.
4,5-dihydro-5-(phenyl)isoxazole derivatives with phenyl rings substituted with chloro and nitro groups are potential antibacterial agents against S. aureus and A. niger.

The antibacterial activity of metal complexes of FMIMDIP was tested against B. thuringiensis, S. pneumoniae, E. coli, and P. putida, with complex 3 showing the best activity among all tested complexes.

The oximino-piperidinopiperidine series includes compounds with antiviral activity, whose activity is optimized by specific substituents at the 4-phenyl position and by the nature of the amide side chain.
Selected 1-(imidazol-4-ylmethyl)-1,4-benzodiazepine analogues demonstrate in vivo antitumor activity in a Rat-1 intraperitoneal/intraperitoneal tumor model.
b-CCM, a benzodiazepine inverse agonist, increases acetylcholine release in the nucleus basalis magnocellularis when administered via microdialysis.
N-methyl-b-carboline-3-carboxamide is a benzodiazepine inverse agonist that stimulates tyrosine hydroxylase activity in the rat prefrontal cortex.
3-(6-phenylimidazo[2,1-b] [1, 3, 4] thiadiazol-2-yl)-1H-indole derivatives are a new series synthesized and evaluated for cytotoxic activity.
4-pyridazone derivatives have been synthesized and tested for chemotherapeutic properties, including activity against tuberculosis.
DMCM, a ␤-carboline, acts as a negative allosteric modulator and inhibits GABA-induced currents at the benzodiazepine site of GABA A receptors in cultured auditory neurons.
Pyrrolyl-1,3,4-thiadiazole derivatives are synthesized by hybridization between thiadiazole and pyrrole rings and have been studied for anti-tuberculosis activity.

1,2-diphenylimidazole derivatives enhance [3H]GABA binding to rat cerebral cortical membranes, with EC50 values ranging from 88 to 12390 nM, and this enhancement correlates with their ability to potentiate GABA-evoked chloride currents.
Benzoindoloquinoxaline derivatives have been synthesized and assessed for anti-VSV activity, interferon-inducing ability, and cytotoxicity.
6-hydroxy-tetrahydroquinoline serves as a scaffold for a new series of estrogen receptor ligands with high binding affinity and functional activity in MCF-7 cells.
2-benzyl-1,3-diphenyl-pyrrolo [1,2-a]quinoline derivatives possess significant antibacterial and antifungal effects and are important synthetic targets for developing new pharmaceuticals.

1-(imidazol-4-ylmethyl)-1,4-benzodiazepine derivatives can revert the transformed phenotype of Ras-transformed cells and prevent anchorage-independent growth in soft agar at concentrations as low as 160 nM.
Mono-fluorination at the methyl-phosphinic group of 3-aminopropylphosphinates reduces affinity for the rat brain GABAB receptor by approximately three times.
Certain benzo[a]pyridazino[3,4-c]phenazine derivatives display antimicrobial activity with IC50 < 6 µM against Staphylococcus aureus and Bacillus subtilis.
Some 1,3,4-oxadiazinone derivatives exhibit antibacterial activity.
Triazafluorenone derivatives have been developed as mGluR1 receptor antagonists with efficacy in several rat in vivo pain models.
Benzoindoloquinoxaline derivatives have been synthesized and assessed for anti-vesicular stomatitis virus (VSV) activity, interferon-inducing ability, and cytotoxicity.

NPY-1 antagonists are a class of compounds that can be identified by decorating the tetrahydrocarbazole scaffold, resulting in compounds with good in vitro potency and promising in vivo pharmacokinetic profiles in rats.
7-[4-((3-hydroxy-4-oxo-4H-pyran-2-yl)methyl)piperazin-1-yl]quinolone derivatives have been synthesized and evaluated for antibacterial activity.
4-oxo-1,4-dihydrocinnoline-3-carboxylic acid derivatives have been synthesized and their in vitro antibacterial activity evaluated.
Replacement of L-daunosamine with L-acosamine in anthracyclines produces analogues with similar antitumour activity but reduced cardiotoxicity.
4-demethyl-6-deoxydoxorubicin is the most cytotoxic anthracycline analogue tested in P388 cells.
2-[2-[bis-(2-chloroethyl)amino]ethyl]-1H-benz[de]isoquinoline-1,3-dione (Naphthalmustine) does not induce hepatotoxicity or nephrotoxicity in mice at the tested dose and schedule.
α-fluoro-β-alanine hydrochloride (FABL), a precursor of fluoroacetate, accumulates in the organism and contributes to delayed cardiotoxicity after 5-fluorouracil-based chemotherapy.
7-(2,3-epoxypropoxy)actinomycin D retains DNA binding property and enhances in vivo tumor-inhibiting activity compared to actinomycin D analogues.
4-[2-(1H-indol-3-yl)vinyl]quinoline does not cause cardiovascular system dysfunction or genotoxicity in mice.
3-amino-4-hydroxymethylacridine is toxic in vivo despite its anticancer activity.
Gemcitabine is a novel antimetabolite with broad activity in preclinical solid tumor models.
7-(2,3-epoxypropoxy)actinomycin D retains DNA binding property and enhances in vivo tumor-inhibiting activity compared to AMD.I0.
2'-deoxy-9-deazaadenosine exhibits pronounced cytotoxicity against several experimental murine and human leukemias.
Lead acridone compounds show feasible safety profiles in in vitro cardiotoxicity and mutagenicity assays, with no overt clinical toxicity or behavioral changes observed in treated mice.
7-Cysteaminomitosane (RR-150) is less leukopenic than mitomycin C in mice.
Anemia and methemoglobinemia are the dose-limiting toxicities of N-(4-chlorophenyl)aminocarbonyl-4-methylbenzene in humans and animals.
2-[2-[bis-(2-chloroethyl)amino]ethyl]-1H-benz[de]isoquinoline-1,3-dione (Naphthalmustine) does not adversely affect hematopoiesis in vivo, as shown by normal hematological parameters in treated mice.
Stilbenemide is an anticancer compound that can induce hepatotoxicity in experimental models.
Ferroptocide exhibits immunostimulatory activity in a murine cancer model.
Platinum-phosphono carboxylate complexes are less kidney toxic than cisplatin, as indicated by lower BUN and serum creatinine levels in treated mice.
Zinecard is a cardioprotective agent that can be administered in conjunction with anthracyclines to extend the upper limit of anthracycline dose and prevent cardiac injury.
Nitrosourethane derivatives are associated with a high frequency of microspherule formation in hepatocyte nucleoli following administration in animal models.
Zinecard is a cardioprotectant agent used concomitantly with anthracycline plus trastuzumab chemotherapy regimens to reduce cardiac toxicity.
Hydroquinone-glutathione is used as a substrate to study hydroquinone metabolism in hepatocytes, including optimization of substrate concentration and incubation time.
The conversion of Hydroquinone-glutathione to a di-glutathione conjugate in hepatocytes requires rate constants that are optimized to account for mass balance in metabolic studies.
DPPD does not affect the induction of peroxisomal acyl CoA oxidase activity by nafenopin in rat hepatocytes.
Tetradecylthiopropionic-CoA facilitates translocation of enzymes from cytosol to microsomes in rat liver cell-free extract in vitro.
Bromotamoxifen does not undergo metabolic activation in rat liver.
Harmol glucuronidation in rat liver is relatively insensitive to hypoxia, but sensitivity increases markedly in fasted animals.
Glucuronidation of Benzo(a)pyrene monophenol does not modulate Benzo(a)pyrene cytotoxicity and is consistent with its negligible mutagenicity.
The conversion of 3H-squalene to sterols by rat liver microsomes and cytosol is inhibited by individual rat and human plasma lipoproteins at various concentrations.
Androstane disulfate metabolism in adult female rat liver is mediated by cytochrome P-450i, which is responsible for the sex-specific microsomal 15γ-hydroxylase system.
Biflavonols are significant in-vitro inhibitors of CYP enzymes in human liver microsomes.
Hydroxylation of 5α-cholestane-3α,7α,12β-triol by liver mitochondria and microsomes from rats is influenced by prior administration of testosterone or oestradiol valerate.

BA-CoA conjugates are formed during bile acid biosynthesis, and their levels in hiHep lysates are lower than in PHH lysates, potentially due to reduced expression of BA-CoA synthetase and BA-CoA:amino acid N-acetyltransferase.
Bilirubin ditaurine does not require Mrp2 for efficient elimination in bile.
2-fluoro-4-[(4-oxo-4,5,6,7-tetrahydro-1H-indole-3-carbonyl)-amino]-phenoxy acetic acid is used as a substrate in 1-hour NADPH-dependent human liver microsome incubations to study its metabolism.

5a-androstane-3a, 17β-diol 17-sulphate undergoes metabolism in isolated hepatocytes involving first-order kinetics (k = 0.021 min⁻¹ x g cells⁻¹) for the disappearance of its monosulphate form, indicating rapid uptake and sulphation of the 3β-hydroxysteroid.
Co-incubation of hepatocytes with DPPD and nafenopin at 300 µM nafenopin results in a higher specific activity of peroxisomal acyl CoA oxidase than with 300 µM nafenopin alone.
The major metabolic pathways for 1-[4-(2-methoxyethyl) phenoxy]-3-[[2-(2-methoxyphenoxy) ethyl]amino]-2-propanol hydrochloride in human liver microsomes are demethylation and hydroxylation.
Glucoerysolin is a major contributor to the negative direction of PC-2 in a model for colonic EROD activity regulation.
Cis-avicennol treatment for 3 days increases Bili-UGT microsomal activity by 1.5- to 2.5-fold in human hepatocytes from donor 1.

4-([4-[(2-hydroxyethyl)methylcarbamoyl]phenyl) quinolin-8-yl-methylene)-1-thiazol-4-yl-methylpiperidinium is a selective δ-opioid receptor agonist whose metabolites have been identified in hepatocyte samples using an IT-OT mass spectrometer.
Lobeglitazone does not significantly affect the pharmacokinetics of metformin or vice versa in healthy adults.
Lobeglitazone, when combined with metformin, provides beneficial efficacy and a favorable safety profile for patients with type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM).
Agonist activity of capsaicin analogues in vitro is predictive of antinociceptive activity in vivo.
High concentrations of MP can inhibit insulin secretion during static incubation, potentially due to acidification of the medium over time.
The effect of MP on membrane potential after 1 hour of perifusion is consistent and cannot be mimicked by pyruvate, supporting its role as an insulinogenic substrate.
2,4-Dichloro-5-(2,4-dichlorophenoxy)benzoic acid inhibits transcriptional activation mediated by PPARg and RXRa in NIH-3T3 cells.
5,7-DHT is toxic to enteric nerves in guinea pigs, indicating species differences in susceptibility.
In adult fathead minnows (Pimephales promelas), EHMC displays endocrine activity as evidenced by alterations in gene expression, VTG plasma concentrations, and gonad histology.
(R)-N-(2-[4- (carboxymethyl)phenoxy]ethyl)-N-(beta-hydroxyphenethyl)ammonium chloride was developed as a potential treatment for obesity and non-insulin-dependent diabetes mellitus.
Livers of rabbits, dogs, and monkeys produce more 2,4-dinitrobenzyl alcohol under aerobic conditions during in vitro incubation with 2,4-DNT.
In the comparison of silicon and carbon analogues, triphenylphenol silicon analogue exhibits a log P value 0.5–0.7 larger than the corresponding carbon analogue.
6,7-methylenedioxy-2,2-dimethylchromene exhibits severe nematocidal effects but does not display precocene-like (P-like) activity in nematodes.
CH3Hg-Hcy is a transportable substrate of hOAT1, as shown by its uptake in MDCK II cells expressing hOAT1.
PCB-153 exhibits pro-estrogenic activities in experimental animal models.
MP acts primarily as an initiator of insulin secretion, likely through mechanisms distinct from K + ATP-channel-independent pathways.
MQSG undergoes greater redox cycling than its parent quinone when incubated with hepatic microsomal fractions.

Methylsulphinyltetrachlorobenzene is metabolized in vivo to form methylsulphinyltetrachlorobenzene-S-peptidase congeners, which are major biliary intermediates and undergo sequential tissue metabolism, biliary excretion, cholangio-enteric metabolism, and enterohepatic circulation.

The mean AUC of 3'-hydroxydiclofenac after a single 150 mg oral dose of diclofenac is significantly different between healthy subjects and patients with alcoholic cirrhosis, but not between healthy subjects and chronic active hepatitis patients.

Pentachlorophenylmethyl sulphoxide can undergo direct displacement of its sulphur moiety by intracellular nucleophiles such as glutathione, resulting in the formation of I4C-methane sulphinic acid in vivo in rats.
DDVP can be routinely monitored in patient plasma using HPLC methods that are sensitive and economical for measuring clinically significant concentrations.
Blockade of neurokinin-2 receptors prevents the induction of central hyperalgesia following noxious stimulation.

Neurokinin-2 receptor antagonists block long-term potentiation (LTP) of C-fibre evoked field potentials in the spinal dorsal horn of adult rats, indicating their role in inhibiting synaptic plasticity induced by noxious stimulation.
TRI achieves steadily rising concentrations in the mouse brain during prolonged inhalation exposure, but this does not prevent the development of behavioral tolerance in operant tasks.
Triethylgermanium acetate does not have a predominant action on the central nervous system in rats.

Repeated exposure of Wistar rats to 20 p.p.m. TBT for 6 hours per day for 14 days produces persistent changes in the amplitudes of flash-evoked potentials, interpreted as increased synaptic activity in the visual pathway or decreased activity in inhibitory interneurons.
N-(1-(2-phenoxyethyl)-4-piperidinyl)propionanilide significantly decreases heart rate, mean arterial pressure, respiratory rate, and neuromuscular transmission in rats.
11beta-PGE2 is a major brain prostaglandin that can be separated and quantified without derivatization using an LC/MS/MS method developed for brain prostaglandin analysis.
Triprolidine hydrochloride at 5 mg causes significant impairment of digit symbol substitution 6 to 7 hours after administration.
Aminocyclopentane dicarboxylic acid (ACPD) is an mGluR agonist that robustly hyperpolarizes area X-projecting HVc neurons in songbirds.

1S,3R-ACPD is a metabotropic glutamate receptor (mGluR) agonist that directly hyperpolarizes HVc neurons, with the hyperpolarization being similar in the presence and absence of the Naϩ channel blocker tetrodotoxin.
Bath application of 1S,3R-ACPD at 100 μm induces a membrane hyperpolarization in HVc neurons, associated with a decrease in input resistance.

Microinjection of (1S,3R)-aminocyclopentanedicarboxylic acid (>5 nl, 1 mM) into the retrotrapezoid nucleus induces respiratory recovery in the ipsilateral phrenic nerve in a dose-dependent manner in rats with left C2 spinal cord hemisection.

Bromoketene reversibly inhibits the response to cooling in subpopulations of cold-sensitive (CMS) neurons by decreasing response amplitude and increasing the threshold temperature required to trigger a calcium response, specifically in neurons responsive to bromoketene.
Toxicological and pharmacological studies in animals and humans indicate that 1'-methyl-spiro (adamantane-2,3'-pyrrolidine) maleate is suitable for oral use in humans.

2-amino-N-(2-{4-[3-(2-trifluoromethyl-phenothiazin-10-yl)-propyl]-piperazin-1-yl}-ethyl)-acetamide trihydrochloride (C-027) is a novel long-acting compound that acts as an antihistamine and antiserotonergic agent.
Butylcaptax intoxication at 1/10 LD50 for 5 days suppresses respiration and oxidative phosphorylation in rat liver mitochondria.
DEPC treatment can inhibit central chemoreception for CO2 and diminish the effectiveness of pirenzepine in inhibiting central chemoreception.
Unlike opioid receptor antagonists, GAL-021 does not reverse or compromise opioid analgesia in rats.
The BN 52063 fraction, at concentrations between 0.25 and 10 pg/mL, does not significantly modify [H]S-HT synaptosomal uptake compared to controls.

Repeated exposure to 50 p.p.m. TBT for up to 127 days causes minor morphological changes in the nervous system, including scattered myelin loss in the corpus callosum, pendunculi, cerebellum, and white matter of the spinal cord in rats.

FTY720 is an immunomodulator that acts as a potent immunosuppressive agent after kidney transplantation in rodents and humans when combined with subtherapeutic concentrations of cyclosporin A, FK506, or rapamycin.

OKY-046 is a specific thromboxane synthetase inhibitor that, when administered to rats with kidney allografts, enables complete preservation of renal function for four days in an acute renal allograft rejection model without other immunosuppressive therapy.
Malononitrilamide FK778 has been investigated as an antirejection drug with potential roles in preventing allograft and xenograft rejection in rodent and dog models.

Therapeutic administration of 4-acetyl-1-(4-chlorophenyl)-3-(2-methylthiophenyl)-3-pyrazolin-5-one prevents the development of autoimmune renal disease in the chronic GvH model of systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) and in NZB/W Fl mice with lupus-like disease.
Resveratrol suppresses T-cell expansion and pro-inflammatory cytokine production in vivo and in vitro.
Liposome-encapsulated dichloroethylene diphosphanate eliminates Kupffer cells in the donor liver, which improves graft function and reduces cytokine production after liver transplantation.
1,2,4-triazolo [4,3-c]pyrimidine derivatives suppress IL-2 production by peripheral blood mononuclear cells and whole blood.
OKY-046, as a thromboxane antagonist, may serve as a potential adjunctive therapy for the treatment of acute renal allograft rejection.
Neocurdione inhibits in vivo liver injury induced by co-administration of TNF and DGalN.

Tetrafluorophenoxy methyl ketone warhead analogues are more potent than other inhibitor classes in reducing hepatocellular apoptosis, plasma ALT elevation, and lethality in an R-Fas-mediated acute liver injury model.
2-Amino-2-{2-[octylphenyl]ethyl}-1,3-propanediol (FTY-720) reduces ischemia-reperfusion injury (IRI) in kidney and liver tissues in vivo.

Tetrafluorophenoxy methyl ketone analogues exhibit dose-dependent reduction of liver injury endpoints in an R-Fas-mediated model, with ED50 values in the mg/kg range depending on administration route.
SK&F 106615 alleviates symptoms of experimental autoimmune encephalomyelitis in Lewis rats.
Haptenated red cell-treated pigs are highly tolerant to I and its congeners throughout an 8-week study period.
Intratracheal delivery of (R)-2-amino-4-(4-heptyloxyphenyl)-2-methylbutanol (AAL-R) inhibits T-cell responses to influenza virus infection in vivo.

4-acetyl-1-(4-chlorophenyl)-3-(2-methylthiophenyl)-3-pyrazolin-5-one suppresses lymph node swelling in the rat graft versus host (GvH) reaction and reduces antibody production and cutaneous hypersensitivity in mice challenged with oxazolone.
In vivo phosphorylation of (R)-2-amino-4-(4-heptyloxyphenyl)-2-methylbutanol (AAL-R) is required for its immunomodulatory effects in the lungs.
Agonist activity of capsaicin analogues in vitro is predictive of antinociceptive activity in vivo.
Olopatadine demonstrates antiallergic activity in vivo.
4-cyanoprogesterone is a potent inhibitor of steroidal 5a-reductase and exhibits modest in vivo potency in ablating androgen-dependent organs in WHT mice.
